series_description: The trial judge of any court of record shall suspend or revoke the driver's license of any person who is convicted, or pleads guilty to, any violation as listed in section 4507.16 of the Ohio Revised Code. The Conviction Cases are records of convictions or bond forveitures (Form UTT - Uniform Traffic Ticket). The tickets contain the violator's name and address, license number, date, location, type of violation, court's findings, sentence imposed and conviction date. The cases also contain insurance certificates or bonds evidencing financial responsibility and all correspondence relating thereto. These documents combine to establish cases which are scanned onto the optical imaging system in the Safety Responsibility Division of the Bureau. NOTE: Effective March 16, 1983, any person of a second offense D.U.I. (driving under the influence of alcohol and/or controlled substances) or any first time major conviction as specified under section 4507.16 of the Ohio Revised Code, is required to pay a reinstatement fee of $125.00 and maintain proof of financial responsibility insurance for 3 years from the date of suspension.
retention_statement: Paper: Effective September 1990 all cases generated will be recorded onto the optical imaging system. All documents are scanned onto the optical imaging system, reviewed ; Microform: Any cases generated prior to January 1989 will remain on microfilm/microfiche if not updated onto the optical imaging system. The microfiche will not be duplicated for storage at the State Records Center. The cases are retained for three (3) years from date of suspension ; Electronic: The disks will be duplicated and stored at the State Records Center. The case will be retained for three (3) years from date of the suspension
disposition: Paper: Wastepaper ; Microform: Burn ; Electronic: Erase

series_title: Exam Registration forms and exam rosters
series_description: Individuals seeking initial certification as a First Responder, EMT-Basic; reinstatement of a certificate as a First Responder, EMT-Basic, EMT-Intermediate, or EMT-Paramedic; challenging an exam in lieu of doing continuing education for any level; and individuals seeking instructor certification must pass an examination approved by the State Board of EMS. These exams are administered by proctors hired by the Division of EMS. Individuals may register for an examination on line or my submitting a hard copy application. The application contains the individuals name, address, date of birth, social security number, type of exam requested, training institution, date and location of requested exam. Exam candidates are converted to exam rosters by exam date and location.

series_title: Exam Results - Exam in lieu of CE / Reinstatement
series_description: Individuals seeking to renew a certificate to practice as a First Responder or EMT, may challenge an examination approved by the State Board of Emergency Medical Services (EMS) in lieu of completing continuing education requirements. (Provided for in O.R.C. 4765.30). The examination approved for each level is provided by the National Registry of EMTs. Answer documents are graded by the NREMTs with exam results reported to the Division of EMS. First Responders and EMTs whose certificate to practice has lapsed by request reinstatement of their certificate. If the certificate has been expired for less than three years, they may challenge an exam approved by the State Board of EMS at the appropriate level. If the certificate has been expired for more than three, but less than six years, they may challenge the exam following completion of remedial training approved by the board. The exams approved by the board for this purpose are provided by and graded by the NREMTs. Exam results are reported to the Division of EMS. Exam results include the name of the individual, mailing address, social security number, exam date, exam level, exam scores.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain permanently. ; Paper: Retain for four years from exam date

series_description: Individuals seeking to obtain a certificate to practice as a First Responder or EMT, must pass an examination approved by the State Board of EMS. (Provided for in O.R.C. 4765.30). The examination approved for each level is provided by the National Registry of EMTs. Answer documents are graded by the NREMTs with exam results reported to the Division of EMS. Exam results are reported by training institution and include the name of the individual, mailing address, social security number, exam date, exam level, and exam scores. Accredited institutions are required by O.A.C. to demonstrate a minimum of 65% passing percentage on the first exam attempt of all students successfully completing training at their program (for each accreditation cycle).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for duration of training institution accreditation cycle for which exam results are applicable (up to three years)

series_description: In accordance with sections 4509 of the Ohio Revised Code, the driver of any motor vehicle which is involved in a motor vehicle accident with an uninsured driver shall, within thirty days forward a written report of the accident (Accident Report) to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles for processing. At that time, the Bureau will evaluate the accident report. If the dollar amount for the property damage cannot be determined or there is no estimate on the report, then a non-accident case is established.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are scanned into image and held for 30 days and ; Electronic: IT should scan files and delete records 1 year from set-up date. All cases generated on/or after January, 1989 are stored in imaging. Retain for 1 year from set-up date. If no additional documentation is received to establish a suspension all records are deleted after 1 year from the set-up date.

series_description: If an individual is involved in a crash, receiveS a citation or IS randomly selected and did not have insurance or proof of financial responsibility at the time of the incident, their driving and registration privileges will be suspended for a period of 90 days to 1 year (if multiple offender). In order to reinstate their driving privileges, they must serve the suspension and pay a reinstatement fee which may range from $30 to $550 based on the date of the incident and prior suspensions for noncompliance. They are also required to file and maintain financial responsibility insurance with the Ohio Bureau of Motor Vehicles in the form of an insurance certificate (SR-22) or a financial responsibility bond for a period of three to five years from the effective date of suspension. Registration privileges may be restored upon receipt of the required reinstatement fee(s) and proof of financial responsibility insurance. This would allow another person to operate the vehicle during the period of license suspension. All incoming correspondence relating to the above suspension are stored in the image case folder.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are scanned into image and held for 30 days and ; Electronic: IT should scan files, if case has been closed for 7 years, delete records. All cases generated on/or after January, 1989 are stored in imaging. Retain for 7 years from close date.

series_description: Any person who is driving a motor vehicle while under the influence of alcohol and/or controlled substance (D.U.I.) and is arrested by a police officer, will be requested, under Section 4511.191 of the Ohio Revised Code to submit to a chemical test to determine the alcohol and/or drug content in his/her system. If a person refuses, a form "Notification of Refusal to Submit to a Chemical Test" is completed and forwarded from the Police Department to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles, Driver license Special Case Division, ALS/Implied Unit for the purpose of suspending the persons driving privileges. Subsequently, a case is generated. The case contains the Notification of Refusal to Submit to a Chemical Test, notice of suspension, receipt of certified mail or registered mail, notice of appeal, court journal entry, certification of record and a copy of correspondence to the offender. The case is microfilmed by the case number. All driver licenses and temporary permits are forwarded from either the police stations or courts to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles. The licenses are retained in the Safety Responsibility Division, Records/License Consolidation Section until termination of the persons suspension.

series_title: End of Day Reports and Universal Registration Docu
series_description: The End of Day Reports and Universal Registration documents are files from the Special Plates Unit of the Registration Section. Each file contains an End of Day Deposit Report as well as all Universal Registration applications and supporting documents. The Universal Registration documents include those forms necessary when issuing: voiture, organizational, non-resident, farm truck, collector, camp bus, senior bus, church bus, disability placards, disability, personalized and reserve plates/validation stickers. #1. Voiture & Organizational: Under Section 4503.18 of the Ohio Revised Code, special license plates may be issued to any United States service organization chartered by Congress for the purpose of operating in a parade and /or for exhibition use of specially equiped motor vehicles. #2. Non-resident: Under Section 4503.04 of the ORC, Ohio non-resident license plates may be issued to a company in another state for the intention of transporting unprocessed agricultural products intrastate. #3. Farm Truck: Under Section 4503.04 of the ORC, a farm truck license plate may be issued to farmers for transporting products, livestock, etc. #4. Collector: Under Section 4503.45 of the ORC, the owner of a collector vehicle may purchase collector license plates. #5. Camp Bus: Under Section 4503.17 of the ORC, camp bus license plates may be issued, as assigned by the Board of Education, for the purpose of transporting children to camp. #6. Senior Bus: Under Section 4503.04 of the ORC, senior license plates may be issued to a bus for the purpose of transporting senior citizens. #7. Church Bus: Under Section 4503.07 of the ORC, the church bus plate may be issued to a church bus that is to be used exclusively to transport members of a church congregation to and from church services and functions or to transport children and their supervisors to and from any camping function sponsored by a nonprofit, tax-exempt charitable organization. #8. Disability Placard: Under Section 4503.44 of the ORC, a disability placard may be issued to an individual with a qualifying disability when a physicians prescription has been submitted and the necessary paperwork provided. #9. Disability Plate: Under Section 4503.44 of the ORC, a disability plate may be issued to an individual with a qualifying disability when the proper documentation from the individuals physician has been submitted. #10. Personalized & Reserved: Under Section 4503.42 of the ORC, a plate may be issued to a registrant with personalized or reserved combination of characters at the request of the customer. Any number of Universal Registration Applications and supporting documents may be included in these files. Each file will contain an End of Day Report and associated registration applications and supporting documents. Supporting documentation may include customer medical information as well as individual financial account data (from receipts).
retention_statement: Electronic: Electronic Files: Effective January 1, 2004 all electronic registration files generated after this date are to be retained for 2 and 1/2 years from the date of issue ; Paper: The End of Day Reports and Universal Registrations and supporting documents shall be maintained for two years after the audit date.

series_description: Under Chapters 4517 and 4738 of the Ohio Revised Code, a person may make application for various licenses issued by the Dealer and Salespersons Licensing Unit. The file contains the following information: 1. Dealer License Applicant Files: When a dealer, leasing dealer and/or salvage dealer wants to establish a place of business, he/she must make application to the BMV. 2. Replacement Plate/Validation Sticker/Registration Card Application: The application is completed by the motor vehicle dealership and forwarded to the Dealers and Salespersons Unit for issuance of a replacement license plate/validation sticker/registration card. The form is used when either the plate, sticker or card is lost, stolen or damaged. The application contains license plate number, validation number, name, address, reason for application (lost, stolen or damaged), police notification and applicants signature. The information is keyed into the computer system for easy reference. 3. Statement of Contract - Required by Section 4517.04 . Applicants for new motor vehicle dealer licenses must submit a copy of their agreement with the manufacturer to sell new motor vehicles. This record series contains copies of franchise agreements from all motor vehicle dealerships and is entered into the Dealers computer system. 4. Canceled Dealer and Salvage Licenses: This file contains applications for salvage, leasing or dealer licenses that were canceled voluntarily by the dealership or canceled by the BMV due to the applicant not following the requirements or non-compliance as stated under Chapters 4517 and 4738 of the Ohio Revised Code. No Motor Vehicle Dealers Board action is taken and their fees are refunded. These records are maintained for proof that the applicant previously applied for either a dealer, leasing or salvage licenses.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 10 years ; Electronic: All files on the Dealers computer system are retained for 10 years after the dealership has gone out of business

series_description: Automobile manufacturers are required to submit to each dealership copies of their warranty agreements for all new motor vehicles. The dealerships will forward copies of these agreements to the BMV, Dealer and Salespersons Licensing Unit for their records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 10 years

series_description: This record series consists of three types of computer generated printouts which are combined to produce the necessary information needed for monitoring the distribution, disposition, corrections and errors pertaining to temporary tags. The record series contains the following printouts: 1. Inventory Correction Audit Trail Printouts - Computer generated listings which are used to make inventory corrections on temporary tags and the accompanying applications. The printouts contain the dealers permit number, business date, item numbers, category code, disposition code, application number, status code, form number and reason for error. 2. Dealer Inventory, Update Error Report, Exception Printouts - Computer generated reports which indicate errors in consecutive numbers or series of the temporary tags and/or applications issued to the various dealerships. The error reports are used as a reference source for updating and correcting the computer files. The printouts contain the batch number, permit number, item number, dealers name, disposition code, application number, issue date, applicants name and reason for error. 3. LEADS Correction, Dealer Reformat Error Reports - Computer generated listings which are used to key corrected information into the LEADS computer system. The printouts contain the batch number, application number, issue date, expiration date, vehicle year, make, type, serial number, certificate of title number, owners name, address and social security number. As information is keyed into the computer system and verified, the correction printouts are batched and boxed for storage.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year for reference purposes

series_title: Automatic Entry Printout (Traffic Report) for Minor
series_description: A printout that is data generated from the Informatiom Technology office as result of traffic offenses submitted by courts who transmit information electronically.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for ninety (90) days

series_description: A Change and Delete Form is completed by the License Support Services Section - Record Clearance Unit for changing information in the data system on an individual''s operator license field, such as:Driver license number, weight, height, sex, issuance date, expiration date, date of birth and social security number. The form can also be used to make changes to the actual driving record. If a change is requested by a court, law enforcement agency, individual or another BMV section, they complete the form and forward it to the License Support Services Section - Record Clearance Unit for key entry into the system. The form is assigned a key number and then stored by run date.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for four ( 4 ) years

series_description: Authorizes the release of your name and address and other non-personal information to businesses and individuals. Form is mailed to License Support Services at the time of a DL/ID transaction.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years

series_title: MINORITY BUS. RPT. FOR EQUAL OPPORTUNITY DIVISION
series_description: Enterprise Stastical Reports. Fiscal year quarterly analysis report of expenditures with minority vendors.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current and subsequent fiscal year in file. After the two year period transfer to storage for five years

series_description: Includes requests from various law enforcement agencies for copies of the digitized photographs taken from the Ohio Driver License database. This correspondence is informative (it does not attempt to influence agency policy).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year

series_title: Unclaimed and Abandoned Junk Vehicle Affidavit
series_description: In accordance with section 4513.60, 4513.62 and 4513.63 of the Ohio Revised Code, law enforcement agencies (as determined in accordance with standards fixed by the Registrar of Motor Vehicles) may request the owner to remove their vehicle that has been abandoned for fifteen days on public or private property. If the owner of the abandoned vehicle does not comply within fifteen days upon receipt of certified notification, the law enforcement agency shall obtain a salvage certificate of title from the Clerk of Court in any county. The affidavit (BMV 3701) contains the name and address of the owner, state, name and address of lienholder (if one), name and address of custodian, and the date. Distribution: Original - Clerk of Courts, Duplicate - Custodian, Triplicate - Law Enforcement Agency, Goldenrod - Title Division.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two years

series_description: In accordance with section 4505.101 of the Ohio Revised Code, the owner of any repair garage or place of storage in which a motor vehicle with a value of less than twenty five hundred dollars (the value of the motor vehicle, as determined in accordance with standards fixed by the Registrar of Motor Vehicles, less expenses incurred by the owner) has been abandoned for fifteen days may request the owner to remove vehicle from their property. If the owner of the abandoned vehicle does not comply within fifteen days upon receipt of certified notification, the repair garage or place of storage shall obtain a certificate of title from the Clerk of Court in any county. The affidavit contains the owner''''s name and address, state, name and address of lienholder (if one), custodian''''s name and address, and the date. Distribution: Original - Clerk of Court; Duplicate - Custodian; Triplicate - Title Division.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years

series_description: In accordance with section 4510.032 of the ORC High school Drop Out Suspensions are set up when the superintendent of a school district forwards BMV form 2840 to our office. The student is subject to suspension for the following reasons: The student is a child of compulsory age and has withdrawn from school and is not enrolled in and attending an approved program to obtain a diploma or its equivalent. The student has been absent without legitimate excuse for more than 10 consecutive school days or at least 15 total school days. The student is suspended or expelled from school for the use or possession of alcohol, a drug of abuse, or alcohol and a drug of abuse. The student is knowingly conveying, or attempting to convey, a deadly weapon or dangerous ordnance, knowingly possessing a deadly weapon or dangerous ordnance on school premises.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are held for 30 days and ; Electronic: IT should scan files and delete records 2 years after the subjects 18th birthday if the case has a closed status. All cases generated on/or after May, 2000 are stored in imaging. Retain for 2 years after 18th birthday.

series_description: In accordance with section 4510.037 C (1) of the Ohio Revised Code, any person against whom at least two but less than twelve points have been charged, may enroll in a course of remedial driving instruction that is approved by the director of public safety. Upon completion of an approved course of remedial driving instruction, the person may apply for a credit of two points on their driving record. The registrar shall not approve any credits for a person who completes an approved course of remedial driving instruction pursuant to a judges order under section 4510.02 of the ORC. In any three year period, the registrar shall approve only one two-point credit per driving record and no more than five two-point credits on a persons driving record during that persons lifetime. All incoming correspondence relating to remedial driving course is stored in the image case folder.
retention_statement: Electronic: All remedial driving instruction courses should be maintained for the person''s lifetime.

series_description: A twelve point suspension results when an individual accumulates 12 points on their driving record within a two-year period. At that time, the driver license is suspended in accordance with Section 4510.037 of the ORC. The individual is required to complete an eight hour remedial driving course, pay a thirty dollar reinstatement fee, file FRA insurance for a period of three to five Years and retake the complete driver license examination. All incoming correspondence relating to an individual suspension is stored in the image case folder.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are placed in the recycle bin 30 days after they are scanned. ; Microform: IT should scan file, if case has been closed for 7 years, delete records. All cases generated on/or after May 2001 are stored in imaging. Retain for 7 years from close date. ; Electronic: In accordance with section 4510.037 of the ORC the above suspensions are established and should be maintained under compliance.
disposition: Paper: Recycle ; Microform: Delete ; Electronic: Shred

series_description: A judgment is a civil action taken as a result of damages or injuries caused in a motor vehicle crash. In order to have your driving and registration privileges reinstated, the individual must satisfy the judgment by paying all damages in full or they must make arrangements for paying the damages. They must also file and maintain financial responsibility insurance with the Bureau of Motor Vehicles in the form of an insurance certificate (SR-22) or a financial responsibility bond for a period of three to five years from the effective date of suspension. All incoming correspondence relating to an individual suspension is stored in the image case folder.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are placed in a recycle bin 30 days after they are scanned. ; Electronic: IT should scan files, if case has been closed for 9 years, delete records. All cases generated on/or after January, 1989 are stored in imaging. Retain for 9 years from close date.

series_description: In accordance with section 4510.31 of the ORC, the Bureau of Motor Vehicles shall suspend the probationary drivers license issued to any person who has committed two to three moving violations prior to the persons eighteenth birthday. In order to comply you must complete a juvenile driver improvement program, pay a thirty dollar reinstatement fee, and at the end of the suspension, purchase a temporary permit packet and retake the complete driver license examination. All incoming correspondence relating to an individual suspension is stored in the image case folder.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are held for 30 days and ; Electronic: IT should scan file, if case has been closed for three years

series_description: Accidents - If an individual is involved in a motor vehicle accident, they may file a crash report (BMV-3303) with the Ohio Bureau of Motor Vehicles within six months after the accident if both of the following apply: 1. There was property damage in excess of $400 or there was personal injury; 2. The driver or owner of the other vehicle did not have insurance or other financial responsibility coverage at the time of the accident. All incoming correspondence relating to the above suspension are stored in the image case folder.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are scanned into image and held for 30 days and ; Electronic: IT should scan files, if case has been closed for 7 years, delete records. All cases generated on/or after January, 1989 are stored in imaging. Retain for 7 years from close date.

series_description: In compliance with Section 4510.037(A) of the Ohio Revised Code, any person who has accumulated one-half (6) or more of the total number of points (12) for which a driver''s license may be suspended, shall be notified by a warning letter (BMV 2817). This warning letter is computer generated by the Data Processing Division of the Bureau of Motor Vehicles and contains all convictions of record, along with the number of points charged for each; licensee''s name and address; key number; file number, and the mailing date. The original letter is mailed to the licensee and a copy imaged.
retention_statement: Electronic: All six point warning letters are retained for 9 years.

series_description: If an individual is involved in a motor vehicle crash and the property damages were more than $400 or bodily injury was sustained, they could have a two-year suspension of their driving and registration privileges. In order to have these privileges reinstated they must meet one of the following requirements in addition to satisfying all reinstatement fees and proof of financial responsibility insurance for a period of three to five years. 1. Submit a State of Ohio Motor Vehicle Crash Report (BMV 3303) signed by their insurance agent which shows they had insurance at the time of the collision, or 2. Submit a release or agreement to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles signed by both parties, or 3. Make a security deposit with the BMV in the full amount of damages. All incoming correspondence relating to the above suspension are stored in the image case folder.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are scanned into image and held for 30 days and ; Electronic: IT should scan files, if case has been closed for 7 years, delete records. All cases generated on/or after January, 1989 are stored in imaging. Retain for 7 years from close date.

series_description: Entries ordering a pretrial suspension are submitted by Ohio courts of record in accordance with section 4511.191 of the Ohio Revised Code. Information is keyed into the driver license system suspending the individuals driving privileges pending the outcome of the court hearing. The form contains the persons name, address, social security number, drivers license number, date of birth, offense date, court code, and court case number.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are placed in a recycle bin 30 days after they are scanned. ; Electronic: IT should scan file, if all cases relative to order have been closed for 7 years delete records. All cases generated on/or after October 2004 are stored in image, retain for 7 years from close date.

series_description: Court journal entries (Modification Orders) are submitted by Ohio courts of record. Documents are used to delete, modify, add and change information on an individuals driving record pertaining to suspensions, revocations, license cancellations, narratives, point assessment and grant limited driving privilege. The journal entry contains the name, address, social security number, date of birth, drivers license number, court name court case number and the reason for the adjustment to the driving record. All documents are scanned and stored in the image system.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper documents are placed in a recycle bin 30 days after they are scanned. ; Electronic: IT should scan file, if all cases relative to order have been closed for 7 years delete records. All cases generated on/or after October 2004 are stored in image, retain for 7 years from close date.

series_title: Valid With & Without Photo Drivers License/IDs
series_description: Record consists of an "Out of State Application For Drivers License"(BMV 2002) and any supporting documentation needed for the issuance of an original, renewal, duplicate, down grade, or commerical valid with or without photo drivers license and identification card in accordance with section 4506.06, 4507.06, and 4507.21 of the O R C. Refunds issued to applicants for out of state requests that were not processed due to time limits or incomplete requests are also included.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper records that are not microfilmed for 7 years ; Electronic: At the time application is made to obtain a drivers license, the information, including signatures, shall be captured and stored electronically. Electronic files shall be retained for a period of eight (8) years, and ; Microform: Retain microfilm for 7 years
disposition: Paper: Recycle ; Electronic: Delete ; Microform: Shred

series_description: Tuition reimbursements paid to ODPS employees for satisfactory completion of approved training and/or educational courses.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 fiscal years plus current

series_description: The inventory form is completed by the Special Plates Division and lists all chargeable and accountable items (license plates, validation stickers and handicap placards) being returned to the BMV Distribution Center for disposition due to obsolete, mixed, defective, error, damage or number duplication. The inventory sheet contains the item type, item code, starting and ending series of what was returned, reason, date, total and the signatures of the releasing and receiving divisions. Distribution: Original - Deputy Registrar Services Division, Audit Section; Duplicate - Deputy Registrar Services Division; Triplicate - BMV Distribution Center; Quadruplicate - Special Plates Division; Quintuplicate - Destroyed - no value; Sextuplicate - Destroyed - no value
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one plate cycle

series_description: The form is completed by the Special Plates Division and lists all chargeable and/or accountable items (license plates, validation stickers, etc.) that were transferred from one BMV division to another. The receipt contains the releasing and receiving divisions, towns, counties, item code, starting and ending numbers of what was transferred, reason for transfer, total, and signatures of the releasing/receiving divisions. DISTRIBUTION: Original - Deputy Registrar Services Division, Audit Section; Duplicate - Deputy Registrar Services Division; Triplicate - Special Plate Division (Releasing Division); Quadruplicate - Receiving Division; Quintuplicate - Destroyed, no value; Sextuplicate - Destroyed, no value.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one plate cycle

series_description: The International Registration Plan (IRP), Article IV, Section 408, requires notifying jurisdictions of apportioned registration applications filed in Ohio. Notification is in the form of IRP Recap/Transmittals print-outs that are monthly reports detailing each commercial truck fleet transaction for each jurisdiction.
retention_statement: Paper: IRP Recap/Transmittal Reports will be retained for the current commercial vehicle registration year plus three previous years records.

series_description: When an original covert, fictitious, disabled veteran (DV), former prisoner of war (POW) or public owned vehicle registration is lost, damaged or stolen, the owner can apply for a duplicate registration. The registration consists of: License number, owners name, address, co-owners name/code, address, vehicle year, make, type, serial number, title number, odometer reading, purchase date, issuance date, expiration date, tax district, county code, previous application number, new application number, deputy name, agency number and location. Distribution: Original copy - DV, POW and public owned registrations are filed by application number in Gratis Unit; covert and fictitious registrations are filed separately by plate number. 1st carbon copy - mailed to applicant. 2nd carbon copy - DV and POW registrations are shredded in Gratis Unit; public owned, covert and fictitious registrations are mailed with the 1st carbon copy.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year after issuance

series_description: When state or political subdivisions acquire vehicles to be used for government or proprietary functions, they apply for vehicle registration and license plates. The registration contains: License number, owners name and address, co-owners name/code, vehicle year, make, type, serial number, title number, odometer reading, purchase date, issuance date, expiration date, tax district, county code, old application number, new application number, deputy name, agency number and location. Distribution: Original copy - Registration is filed by application number in Gratis Unit with application. 1st and 2nd carbon copy - Mailed to applicant.
retention_statement: Paper: Registration is retained for 1 year after issuance date in the Gratis Unit ; Microform: Retain for 7 years after issuance date

series_description: In cases where disciplinary action is contemplated and the affected employee elects to participate in the Employee Assistance Program (EAP), the disciplinary action may be delayed until completion of the program. An employee may volunteer to participate in the EAP program without any discipline being imposed. If an employee goes through HRM for assistance, the OEAP participation agreement packet is completed by the employee and the employer, and forwarded to EAP for his/her treatment. The packet is NOT retained in the employee personnel file, but kept in an HRM confidential file.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years after completion of treatment, or longer if needed due to administrative reasons.

series_title: Disabled Veteran/Former Prisoner of War Correspondence
series_description: Correspondence received in the Gratis Unit from disabled veteran (DV) applicants and former prisoner of war (POW) applicants interested in obtaining grants toward the purchase of a motor vehicle, seeking eligibility for gratis plates, regarding returned replacement notices, out of state notices, deceased notices, etc. Correspondence is filed on site by applicants name. Distribution: Original correspondence is filed with the application.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year in Gratis Unit

series_title: DAV/Former POW/Cong Medal of Honor registration app
series_description: Registrations are issued for 100% service connected disabled veterans (DV) and/or former prisoner of war (POW), and medal of honor recipients with VA or military documentation. The former POW plate is available to the surviving spouse with an application and a certified copy of the death certificate. The registration is generated from the application with the title and military proof when new, transfer, replacement and corrections are issued on the following vehicles: Non-commercial trucks, passenger vehicles, motor homes, motorcycles, boat trailers, etc. The registration contains the license plate number, owner name, address, vehicle year, make, type, serial number, title number, odometer reading, purchase date, issuance date, expiration date, tax district, county code, previous application number, new application number, deputy name, agency number and location. Distribution: Original copy - Filed by form number in Gratis Unit, 1st carbon copy - Mailed to applicant with plate, 2nd carbon copy - Shredded in Gratis Unit.
retention_statement: Paper: Registrations are retained for 1 year in the Gratis Unit ; Microform: Retain for 7 years after issuance date

series_description: Fictitious registrations are issued for unmarked vehicles used only for investigation and surveillance undercover work. The registration is generated when new, transfer, replacement or renewal plates are issued on commercial/non-commercial truck, passenger vehicles, motor homes, motorcycles, trailers, etc. Fictitious plate registration applications are filed as new, transfer, replacement, or renewal. The registration contains: License plate number, fictitious name and address, actual vehicle year, make, type, serial number, title number, odometer reading, purchase date, issuance date, expiration date, tax district, fictitious county code, old application number, new application number, fictitious deputy name, agency number, and location. The application contains: Fictitious name and address, vehicle year, make, type, serial number and title number.
retention_statement: Paper: After audited, retain for 2 years in the Record Center

series_description: Remittance processing reports are computer generated printouts received from Data which lists all registration applications and money issued through the Remittance Processing system. These reports include REG51 (refund reports), REG52 (refund rejects), REG54 (over/under reports), REG57 (rejects reports), REG59 (reject transaction letters), REG60 (errors in generating letters), REG61 (suspended records), and REG62 (accepted records). The printouts contain plate numbers, payment dates, social security numbers, check amounts, donations, amounts due, refund amounts, batch/sequence numbers, refund amounts, error codes, and over/under fees. NOTE: A new procedure went into effect May 2002 - "BMV Remittance Processing". All mail-in applications are processed through the BMV Remittance Processing Unit.
retention_statement: Paper: The reports are retained until audited by Auditor of State and audit report released

series_title: Covert Plate Registrations Applications and Corres.
series_description: Record series consists of covert license plate applications, registrations, and related correspondence. Covert registrations are issued to city, county, township, state or federal government agencies for unmarked vehicles after a certification for undercover plates has been completed and signed by an applicant agencies approved official and the Registrar. Vehicles issued covert license plates are to be used only for investigations and undercover surveillance. The registration is generated when new, transfer, replacement or renewal plates are issued on commercial/non-commercial truck, passenger vehicles, motor homes, motorcycles, trailers, etc. Covert plate registration applications are filed as new, transfer, replacement, or renewal. The registration contains: License plate number, government agency name/code, address, vehicle year, make, type, serial number, title number, odometer reading, purchase date, issuance date, expiration date, tax district, county code, old application number, new application number, deputy name, agency number, and location. The application contains: Government agency name, address, vehicle year, make, type, serial number and title number co-owner name/code and address.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year in Gratis Unit

series_description: As quoted under section 4503.04 Paragraph I of the Ohio Revised Code, when a person or agent of a corporation request registration for a transit bus, the Transit Bus Registration Application (BMV4607 or BMV4619) has to be accompanied with an affidavit. The Transit Bus Affidavit is furnished by the Special Plates Division and signed by the person or agent who will be operating the bus. The person will agree that the bus will have a seating capacity of more than seven persons; be operated and used in a public mass transportation service (and at least seventy-five percent of the annual mileage of such operation); used within one or more municipal corporation(s) or solely for the transportation of persons associated with a charitable or nonprofit corporation. The affidavit is valid for one registration year and a new one is required each year in order to renew registration.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two (2) years along with application.

series_description: Record series consists of a form letter sent to record owners requesting approval for the destruction of records stored in the ODPS Record Center, lists of records to be destroyed, and responses from record owners.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 10 years after disposal of records. ; Electronic: Information is maintained in the Inventory Management System (BIMS) for 10 years after disposal of records.

series_description: In accordance with section 4503.44 of the Ohio Revised Code, a person may apply for a disability placard if they meet at least one of the following conditions: 1. Cannot walk two hundred feet without stopping to rest; 2. Cannot walk without the use of or assistance from a brace, cane, crutch, another person, prosthetic device, wheelchair or other assistive device; 3. Is restricted by lung disease to such an extent that the persons forced (respiratory) expiratory volume for one second, when measured by spirometry, is less than one liter, or the arterial oxygen tension is less than 60 millimeters of mercury on room air at rest; 4. Uses portable oxygen; 5. Has a cardiac condition to the extent that the persons functional limitations are classified in severity as Class III or Class IV according to standards set by the American Heart Association; 6. Is Severely limited in the ability to walk due to an arthritic, neurological, or orthopedic condition; 7. Blind. Applicant may apply to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles, Registration Division (Mail In Unit) or any Deputy Registrar Agency for issuance of a disability placard which entitles the individual to park a motor vehicle in any special parking location reserved for disabled persons. The applicant must complete the application stating name of the disabled person, signature of disabled person or next of kin, street address, city, state, zip, county, social security number, date signed and telephone number. In addition the applicant must submit a prescription from his/her physician or chiropractor stating the disabled persons name, current date, that a disability placard is being requested, the duration of the disability, and the physicians or chiropractors signature. Disability Placards expire on the date stated on the prescription, and must be renewed accordingly. Processing fees are $3.50 per placard. (Applicant’s may apply for one additional placard by stating a reason as to why two placards are needed.) For renewal, an “Application for Renewal of Parking Placards for Persons with Disabilities” is mailed to the customer 45 days prior to the expiration date. The application must be signed by the applicant or next of kin. A prescription from the physician or chiropractor stating the above information is required for renewal.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain six (6) years

series_description: Fiscal year analysis of projected agency expenditures with minority business vendors.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current and previous fiscal year in file. After the two year period transfer to storage for five years

series_title: Complaints aganist commercial motor carriers form
series_description: (HP-130) The form will be used when a citizen, employee of a motor carrier or outside law enforcement agency reports a motor carrier related complaint or violation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper copy until scanned into an electronic format. Review form ; Electronic: Forward electronic copy to the PUCO, Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration and to any affected OSP personnel. Retain electronic file for one year plus current

series_description: When a BMV Field Representative visits a deputy registrar office, he/she completes a Site Visitation Report (SVR) as the reports and functions of the agency are reviewed. The completed report is forwarded via the laptop computer to an Assistant Chief or Chief for review. The Field Representative also completes a Daily Activity Report (FRDAR). The report is updated daily. The report includes dates, times, locations and general tasks performed each day. The FRDAR covers the same two weeks as the pay period and is submitted to the Assistant Chief or Chief for review. The Field Representative also completes an Observation Report for each Saturday unannounced visit to a license agency. The report is filed in the district office and a copy is given to the deputy registrar.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for six years after file is inactive ; Electronic: Retain copy at Deputy Registrar and District Office for two years

series_description: Record series consists of all records that deputy registrars maintain in accordance with section D of the Deputy Registrar Manual. These include End of Day Reports, consignment Invoices (BMV1511), Missing Affidavits (BMV5702), Chargeable and Non-Chargeable Inventories, Error Correction Worksheet (BMV5716), correspondence with BMV Revenue Mangement section, Broadcasts, Evaluations, Work schedule, contracts, motor voter transmission forms, Abstract file, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Contains information collected on employees of a deputy registrar agency during the course of a background investigation. Also includes correspondence from the Ohio Bureau of Criminal Identification and Investigation (BCII).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in active files until the individual terminates employment. Transfer to inactive files for three years

series_description: During the Ohio State Fair, the Bureau of Motor Vehicles has a display setup for sales and information. The Agency permits the general public to renew their vehicle registrations, driver licenses, and identification cards; also, driving abstracts (printout of individual''s driving records) are issued. The record series consists of Statement of Deposits - Form 20, Application for Driver Licenses (Deputy Registrar''s copy), Application for Identification Cards (Deputy Registrar''s copy), and Vehicle Registrations (Deputy Registrar''s copy).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for four years

series_description: Records are comprised of Deputy Registrar copies of Ohio Driver License/Identification card Applications (form BMV 2002) , signed Proof of Financial Responsibility (form BMV 3135), and other supporting documents. Record is a duplicate of documents maintained by Bureau of Motor Vehicles via schedule GK/MGK.
retention_statement: Paper: Hard copies are retained for four years from date of issuance in accordance with the Deputy Registrar Manual. ; Electronic: Machine readable copies are retained for seven years from date of issuance

series_description: The evaluations are completed by Field Services representatives annually and becomes part of the field audit report. The evaluation contains the name of the deputy registrar, city, county, agency number, date, summary of evaluation, comments, recommendations, special problems, and the field representatives name. DISTRIBUTION: Original - Field Services District Office, Copy - Deputy Registrar.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in active files for three years

series_description: Audit correspondence covers the results of audits of agency chargeable inventories. Revenue Management correspondence addresses depositing errors such as shortages, overages, missing signatures, bank stamps and reversal applications being keyed incorrectly.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in active files until periodic evaluation is completed. Transfer to inactive files for three years

series_description: Incident reports are used to document facts and circumstances surrounding an event or occurrence, i.e. in the case of a customer complaint, the report would contain the name and telephone number of the individual.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in active files at district offices for three years

series_description: OCJS Grant payment vouchers to various agencies receiving federal grants.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 years after close of grant

series_description: An annual inventory is taken by each Deputy Registrar on all chargeable and accountable items such as: license plates, validation stickers, county stickers, temporary tags, universal vehicle registration applications, driver license applications, and identification card applications. The report contains the agency number, Deputy Registrars name, registration year, business year, and the different types of inventory - validation sticker inventory; county sticker inventory; recreational vehicle stickers, temporary tags, applications and monthly expiration stickers inventory; and the plate inventory. Each inventory is broken-down by the type of item, starting number, ending number and the total. There is also inventory sheets listing all items that were picked up and returned to the BMV Distribution Center. If returned to the Distribution Center, the employee receiving the items will sign and date the inventory report. After the inventory has been completed, the report is signed and dated by the Deputy Registrar and the BMV field representative. The report is then submitted to Central Operations/Audit & Consignment Section (CO/ACS). Distribution: Original - CO/ACS; Duplicate - BMV Field Representative; Triplicate - Deputy Registrar.
retention_statement: Paper: Rertain for a minimum of 3 years

series_description: As defined under section 4503.44 of the Ohio Revised Code, any person who has lost the use of one or both legs or one or both arms, who is blind, deaf, or so severly handicapped as to be unable to move without the aid of crutches or a wheelchair, or whose mobility is restricted by a permanent cardovascular, pulmanary, or other handicapping condition may apply for handicap license plates. A form "Physicians Certification of Eligibility for Handicap License Plates" must be completed by the applicant and physician. This form is then submitted to the Special Plates Division along with the Registration Application. The form contains the applicants name and address, physicians signature, physicians name, physicians license number and address. NOTE: The Physician Certification is a one-time submission.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year after placard has expired.

series_title: Portable and Platform Scale Activity Recap
series_description: To record portable and platform scale activity generated for each month. (HP-128)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year plus current

series_title: CONVICTION TICKETS (UNIFORM TRAFFIC TICKETS) - MAJOR TICKETS
series_description: Under section 4510.03 of the Ohio Revised Code, conviction tickets are received from the county court of record or mayors court resulting from violations of law or ordinance regulating the operation of a motor vehicle on the streets and highways. These are all major convictions (6 point traffic tickets, under sections 4507.16, 2903.06, 2903.07 and 4511.191 of the ORC, and any traffic tickets specified under sections 4549.021 and 4549.03 of the ORC. The tickets contain the violators name and address, drivers license number, license plate number, date, date of birth, name of the court, court case number, nature of offense, date of hearing, number of points charged, suspension date, fine, violators social security number, and the signature of the court clerk. In accordance with the provisions of section 4507.16 Division (B)(2) of the ORC, the suspension period shall be totaled on a five year basis from date of first conviction.
retention_statement: Paper: The hard copies of the records are destroyed after being scanned on the Optic Image System. ; Electronic: Major convictions are stored on the Optic Image System for 25 years and on the person''''s driving record for 10 years.

series_description: Under section 4507.08 of the Ohio Revised Code and Administrative Code, Chapter 4501, Sections 4501:1-1-16 and 4501:1-1-17, state that any person who is convicted three (3) or more times within the immediately preceding three (3) year period of any combination of driving under the influence of intoxicating liquor or a controlled substance (D.U.I.) shall have his/her driving privileges suspended. At that time, a case is established. Each case contains copies of Notification of Suspension form, BMV 2331, Reinstatement Form, BMV 2326, and correspondence. The offenders driving priviledges are suspended until such time the Bureau receives the required clearance. The cases are microfilmed or imaged numerically by an assigned case number.

series_description: File contains original registration, renewal, and transfer applications for Private Investigators and Security Guards. Registration - Under Ohio Revised Code 4749.06, providers must register each employee using form HLS0012. Three types of registrations are completed: new hire/rehire, name change, and card replacement. Under Ohio Revised Code 4749.10, providers must register each employee that intends to carry a weapon (FAB) using form HLS0069. Two types of FAB registrations are completed: intial firearm bearer and requalification firearm bearer. Included with this application will be any necessary paperwork, such as, but not limited to: BCI/FBI results, FAB certification, correspondence, and refund/invoices. Renewal - Under Ohio Revised Code 4501:5-1-20, providers must renew employee registrations annually using form HLS0027. Included with this application will be any necessary paperwork, such as, but not limited to: any correspondence and refund/invoives. Transfer - Under Ohio Revised Code 4749.06, providers must register each employee when they change branches using form HLS0013. Included with this application will be any necessary paperwork, such as, but not limited to: any correspondence and refund/invoices. These records were under jurisdiction of the Department of Commerce until July 1, 2004 (SAN 18000710)
retention_statement: Paper: Applications are retained in the PISG Licensing and Registration Unit for 1 year ; Electronic: Applications are retained in the PISG Licensing and Registration Unit for 1 year ; Microform: Applications are retained in the PISG Licensing and Registration Unit for 1 year

series_description: Sections 4507.163, 4507.19 and 4507.30 of the Ohio Revised Code, states that if a person of insufficient age purchases liquor and displays proof that he/she is of legal drinking age (21 years old) and knowing that the drivers license or permit was altered or to be fictitious, shall forfeit all driving privileges. The permit or license is forwarded from the court to the Driver License Division, Special Case Services Section for processing. A letter is submitted from the Liquor Control Commission to the Bureau for appropriate action. The persons driving privileges are canceled and then placed under suspension for a one (1) year period. After the suspension has been lifted, the applicant will have to be re-tested for a new license.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper records are scanned into the Optic Image System and ; Electronic: Maintained for five years after the case is closed.

series_description: In accordance with section 2937.221 of the Ohio Revised Code, any person arrested under specific traffic violation may post bond by depositing a current valid drivers license to the arresting officer or the court. If the person arrested fails to appear in court at the given time, the license of such person is then forwarded from the court to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles. The BMV notifies the person no valid drivers or commercial drivers license shall be issued to the person after the suspension unless the court having jurisdiction orders the termination of the forfeiture.

series_description: Under section 4513.263 of the Ohio Revised Code, tickets are received from courts of record and/or mayors courts resulting from a licensed driver and/or a front seat passenger not wearing a restraining device (seat belt) when picked up for a traffic violation. NOTE: The traffic tickets (for seat belts) are keyed into the data computer and no points are charged against the persons driving record. Tickets are keyed only for the driver.
retention_statement: Paper: The hard copies of the records are destroyed after being scanned on the Optic Image System. ; Electronic: Seat belt tickets are mainatined on the Optic Image System for five years

series_description: In accordance with section 4507.08 of the Ohio Revised Code, if an individual (first time applicant or new driver) applies for an Ohio drivers license, a computer check is performed thru the Problem Driver Pointer System to verify the status of the persons driving record. If the person is suspended in the other state, Ohio will establish a case and notify the driver he/she has thirty (30) days to comply with the requirements in the other state. He/she must either present a clearance letter or the Problem Driver Pointer System must be clear. NOTE: This law went into effect August 1986 under 4507.08 (D) of the Ohio Revised Code.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper records are scanned and ; Electronic: Three years

series_description: In accordance with sections 4510.71 and 4510.72 of the Ohio Revised Code, any Ohio licensed driver who is issued a citation for a traffic violation from another state and does not comply with the requirements of the citation (pay the ticket), the court from that state will forward a copy of the ticket and the persons driver license to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles, Driver License Division for processing. The Bureau will notify this person, in writing, stating that the delinquent fine must be paid to the out of state court within twenty (20) days or he/she will be placed under suspension. If the person does not comply, the Bureau will then suspend their driving privileges. There are forty (40) states covered under the "Nonresident Violator Compact Law of 1977". Ohio began participating January 1985. THE PERSON WILL REMAIN SUSPENDED UNTIL WE ARE IN RECEIPT OF PAYMENT RECEIPT AND REINSTATEMENT FEE.
retention_statement: Paper: All hard copy cases closed before March 31, 1990, will be retained at agency storage for three years ; Microform: Effective April 2, 1990 all active cases generated before or after this date will be on microfilm/microfiche; any closed cases generated after this date will be on film. Microfilm retained for three years after case is closed. ; Electronic: Non-resident violator cases are stored on the Optic Image System until all requirements are met and
disposition: Paper: Shred ; Microform: Shred ; Electronic: Delete

series_description: In accordance with 4510.61 of the Ohio Revised Code, the licensing authority of a party state shall report each conviction of a person from another party state occuring within its jurisdiction to the licensing authority of the home state of the licensee. series_description: In accordance with section 4506.13 Division B of the Ohio Revised Code, if an individual (a first time commercial driver or a new resident) applies for an Ohio commercial driver license at a Highway Patrol Examination Station or a Deputy Registrar, a computer check is made through the National Driver Register and through the Commercial Driver License Information System, for verification of the applicant''''s driving record. If records indicate that the person is under suspension either in Ohio or another state, the Examination Station or the Deputy Registrar will forward to the Driver License Special Case CDL/OSP Section, either the Application For Temporary Instruction Permit. The applicant will have sixty (60) days to comply with the necessary requirements. The Bureau would need a clearance letter from the other state''''s Bureau of Motor Vehicles showing that the suspension was terminated and the driving privileges reinsated in that state or the Commercial Driver License Information System must be cleared.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper records are scanned and ; Electronic: All Commercial Driver License cancellations and clearances are maintained on the Optic Image System, effective December 2001. Electronic records are maintained for 55 years.

series_description: These records are comprised of correspondence associated with what happens when a person driving a commercial motor vehicle is stopped by a police officer for any of the offenses listed under section 4506.16 Division (A) thru (H) of the Ohio Revised Code, he/she may be disqualified from driving a commercial motor vehicle. If a person is to be disqualified from driving a commercial vehicle, the Bureau of Motor Vehicles would be notified by receiving a judgement entry from the court, administrative order from a state agency having statutory jurisdiction over the commercial driver, computer record obtained from either the National Driver Register or the Commercial Driver License Information System, computer record obtained through a state agency and/or the refusal to a chemical test received from a police station.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper records are scanned and ; Electronic: Electronic records are retained for 55 years.

series_description: The record contains correspondence related to section 4510.17. At the end of suspension, if ninety days or more, the person is required to pay a $30 reinstatement fee in accordance with section 4507.45. Record includes abstract from another state and, indicating conviction - DUI or drug offense, the state in which the offense occurred. There may also be an administrative hearing.

series_description: Prowatch is a security management software used to create access cards (or badges) for all ODPS and ODOT employees as well consultants working for either agency to access ODPS and ODOT buildings. Examples of ODPS buildings include Shipley, Alum Creek, Patrol Academy and Centre School. Each time an employee or consultant swipes their badge at an external card reader used to gain access to a building or an internal card reader used a to gain access to a secure area within a building, the record of the ''swipe'' is recorded in the Prowatch system. Specific data recorded each time the card is swiped includes who swiped, when it occurred, which door was swiped and if the swipe was success (not all individuals have access to all doors or during all times). No personal or confidential data is recorded as part of the swipe. The data that is stored is used mostly during internal investigations dealing with attendance or employees/consultants tardiness issues. The system does not track visitors to any physical location or their movement throughout the building. Currently, a record retention schedule has not been established for this data. After conferring with ODPS Legal, I.T., the Security Operations Group (IT) and the Ohio State Highway Patrol, it is the belief that this data should be retained for the current year plus one historical year.
retention_statement: Electronic: One year plus Current

series_title: Application for Renewal Registration by Mail
series_description: Under Section 4503.10 of the Ohio Revised Code, any owner of a motor vehicle who operates on the streets and highways shall file each year with a deputy registrar of the Bureau of Motor Vehicles an application for vehicle registration (BMV4603, BMV4616, or BMV4617). The computer-generated form is signed and dated by the applicant for registration issuance by mail. The form contains the owners name and address; plate category; type of plate; whether a power of attorney form is required; EPA inspection required if residing in Butler, Clark, Clermont, Cuyahoga, Geauga, Greene, Hamilton, Lake, Lorain, Medina, Montgomery, Portage, Summit, or Warren counties; owners social security number or tax identification number; taxing district/name; purchase date; old application number; new license number; registration expiration date; renewal expiration date; vehicle type, year and make; VIN number; title number; amount paid; dontation (in dollars and cents amount) to Save Our Sight campaign; space to fill out any changes in applicants name or address; applicants signature and date. The applications are imaged according to issuance dates and batch control numbers. Power of attorney forms and EPA inspection certificates are also imaged. EPA inspection certificates are stored with the applications, and the power of attorney forms are stored in separate boxes using the same batch numbers as the applications they belong with. Under Senate Bill 1, in December 1988, the "Mail-In Registration" went into effect. Effective May 2002, all mail in applications are processed through the BMV Remittance Processing Unit. Individuals have a choice to register their motor vehicles by a deputy registrar, mail-in registration, using the OPLATES.com website, or the BMV automated telephone service.
retention_statement: Paper: All applications and supporting documents generated will be retained until imaged and verified. The hard copy records are ; Electronic: Imaged records are retained for three (3) years after the date of issuance

series_description: This record series includes initial and renewal application files for Motorcycle Ohio Program (MOP) training site providers and instructors as well as surveys to improve program development. MOP0033 (2/13): MOP instructor Refresher Workshop Evaluation form; this is a survey collected at the Instructor Refresher Workshop. The survey questions are completed by the certified instructors and evaluated by the program staff and chief instructors. The survey summary is to evaluate instruction and improve instructor’s professional development. MOP0043 (11/11): Application to approve or renew a certification of approval to act as a training site provider for MOP, which includes the following documents: criminal abstract provided by the Bureau of Criminal Identification; proof of liability insurance; and Motorcycle Safety Foundation (MSF) certification for approval. MOP0044 (07/07): Application to approve or renew a certification of approval to act as an instructor for MOP, which includes the following documents: a statement affirming that applicant meets the requirements for certification to teach; criminal abstract provided by the Bureau of Criminal Identification and Investigation; current, valid first aid card and CPR card or equivalent; evidence of successful completion of an instructor preparation course and the basic rider course: applicant’s driving record, and course instruction observation and evaluation sheets; and if applicable, physician’s statement; and MSF certification.
retention_statement: Paper: Until certification lapses, changes, or expires, plus three years. ; Electronic: Until certification lapses, changes, or expires, plus three years.
