retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus one year. ; Electronic: Retain current, plus one year.

series_description: The project management artifacts describe the documentation that aids in the management of a project. Examples of project management artifacts include, but are not limited to, the IT Project Request Business Case (DPS 0240), project charter,scope statements, project plans (including communications plan scope management plan, cost management plan, quality management plan,etc.),scope statement, project estimates and project actuals, project communications, risk log, issue log, status reports, and other related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after project is closed, or the decision is made not to implement the project and ; Electronic: Retain 3 years after project is closed, or the decision is made not to implement the project and

series_title: International Registration Plan (IRP) Permit Agency and Agent Audits
series_description: Account records for permit agencies and agents authorized to process and issue temporary credentials (72-hour trip permits) under the International Registration Plan undergo regular audits for compliance to all requirements for processing and issuing IRP permits. This record series contains audit checklists, audit reports, and all supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for current contract period plus one year. Records may be imaged. If imaged, paper record may be destroyed, once the electronic record is validated. ; Electronic: Retain for current contract period plus one year. These records are retained in the BMV/IRP section''s shared drive folders.

series_description: Contains photos of license plate numbers of passing vehicles that are matched against LEADS, NCIC, and various federal and state databases.
retention_statement: Electronic: All captures are retained for 90 days. Captures related to a case investigation may be retained longer.

series_title: Private Investigator/Security Guard (PISG) Registrant Identification Cards
series_description: Files contain expired and/or terminated identification cards for registered private investigators or security guards.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy upon return from the company or individual.

series_title: Private Investigator Security Guard (PISG) Licensee Status Reports
series_description: Record series includes Insurance Acords (required proof of insurance for the company); Termination Reports (required when a company terminates an employee); and Quarterly Reports (required list of registrants employed by the licensee).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in PISGS office files for the current quarter, plus two years after date of receipt. ; Electronic: Retain in PISGS office files for the current quarter, plus two years after date of receipt.

series_title: Ohio Emergency Management Agency - Emergency Operations Plans
series_description: State, county, and other associated emergency operations plans, including but not limited to, all supporting documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of administrative value or superseded. Contact State for review prior to disposal. ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value or superseded. Contact State Archives for review prior to disposal.

series_title: Private Investigator Security Guard fingerprint background check results
series_description: Contains fingerprint background check results from BCI&I and FBI for private investigator and security guard licensing and registration.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year from date of receipt. ; Electronic: Retain for one year from date of receipt.

series_title: Private Investigator Security Guard Section (PISGS) Enforcement Unit Investigation Reports
series_description: This series may contain, but is not limited to: notice of violation; complaint; PISGS Enforcement Investigator summary report; Qualifying Agent, Registrant, Licensee and/or fire-arm bearer applications; photo identifications and social security numbers; correspondence with DPS Legal Services; and all supporting documentation for the investigative case.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus four years following the date the case is closed, all appeals have run, and all terms of settlement agreements, if applicable, have been fulfilled. ; Electronic: Retain current, plus four years following the date the case is closed, all appeals have run, and all terms of settlement agreements, if applicable, have been fulfilled.

series_title: Ohio Emergency Management Agency - Exercise Records
series_description: All records related to exercises of any part of the state emergency operations plan or other emergency response plans, including but not limited to manuals, program/exercise guidance, exercise scenarios, evaluations, notes, correspondence, after-action reports, corrective action reports, and corresponding presentation materials.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current year plus three years from the date of the federal "Final Financial Report" for the applicable federal grant, and then, until no longer of administrative value. ; Electronic: Retain current year plus three years from the date of the federal "Final Financial Report" for the applicable federal grant, and then, until no longer of administrative value.

series_title: Ohio Emergency Management Agency - Mitigation Records
series_description: All records concerning federal mitigation projects which document the expenditure of, use of and accounting for grant funds, including but not limited to plans, planning guidance, grant guidance, agreements, budgets, cash requests and supporting materials, audit reports and project information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current year plus three years from the date of the federal "Final Financial Report" for the applicable grant, and until the State Single Audit has been completed, and ; Electronic: Retain current year plus three years from the date of the federal "Final Financial Report" for the applicable grant, and until the State Single Audit has been completed, and

series_title: External individual, agency, and organization training records
series_description: All records related to training provided by the Ohio Department of Public Safety (ODPS) to external individuals, agencies, and organizations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current year plus three years from the date of training. If training is related to grant funding, the date of the federal "Final Financial Report" for the applicable grant. ; Electronic: Retain current year plus three years from the date of training. If training is related to grant funding, the date of the federal "Final Financial Report" for the applicable grant.

series_description: The daily requisition report contains special plates orders and is produced via Special Plates Ordering Tracking System (SPOTS). This record series contains the daily documentation which includes the various SPOTS reports, Requisition Committee''''''''s approval or denial of plate selection, appeal documentation, recall information and plate issuance justification. The record will also contain a copy of SPOTS reports confirmation and any email traffic/discussion from the customer or between the Requisition Committee members and the BMV Legal Department.
retention_statement: Paper: The daily requisition reports contained in this series will be stored on site (Shipley) for 1 year from the date of issuance. ; Electronic: The daily requisition reports and correspondence received and/or distributed, contained in this series, will be stored electronically (via Microsoftt Outlook) for 1 year from the date of issuance.

series_description: This record series contains the Application for Leasing/Online Registration (BMV 4630) and the DETER (Drivers with excessive tickets excluded from registration) System Application (BMV 1162), which allow vehicle leasing companies to apply for and maintain powers of attorney and authorize vehicle lessees to make application for registration for their leased vehicles.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the vehicle information services office files until superseded or obsoleted.

series_description: This record series contains original certificates evidencing an OSHP sworn employee''s successful completion of training to use breathalyzers to measure blood alcohol content (BAC).
retention_statement: Paper: Length of service, plus 25 years. Retain initial and all renewal certificates in office files at employee''s assigned post or district for length of service. Upon employee''s separation, forward certificates to DPS HCM to be retained until full retention period elapses.

series_description: This record series contains original certificates evidencing an OSHP sworn employee''s successful completion of training to use speed measuring devices (SMDs) during speed enforcement.
retention_statement: Paper: Length of service, plus one year. Retain original certificate and renewals in office files at employee''s assigned post or district.

series_description: This record series contains: 1) Investigator''s report and supporting evidence collected during investigation, e.g. audio or video files;2) Print-out of current disciplinary (deportment) history record; 3) Disciplinary determination and copy of personnel action, if it is required; This record may also include: 1) Pre-disciplinary notice; 2) discipline substitution notice; 3) Pre-disciplinary waiver; 4) Hearing Officer's report; 5) Settlement (Last chance/Abeyance) agreement; and/or 6) Copy of grievance case file documents including final arbitration award/decision, if discipline is overturned. NOTES: Settlement agreements with terms extending beyond the retention period established in this schedule will be retained with individual's personnel file. OSHP auxiliary member administrative investigation records are retained in the Office of Strategic Services in accordance with this schedule.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years plus current and ; Paper: Maintain in office files until scanned and uploaded in EIR

series_description: Per the Immigration Reform and Control Act (1986), the I-9 Employee Verification Form establishes an employee''s eligibility to work within the United States. These forms are considered part of an employee''s personnel record.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current (originals only -electronic record not acceptable), plus 3 years from the date of employee''s separation.

series_description: Contains the history of the formal discipline imposed on an employee. Formal disciplinary action that is imposed for just cause consists of: One or more written reprimands, a fine in an amount not to exceed five (5) days pay, and one or more days suspension. Prior disciplinary record is used to determine appropriate level for any future discipline.
retention_statement: Electronic: Written reprimands are retained 24 months after the date of the reprimand if there has been no other discipline imposed during the past 24 months. Fines and suspensions are retained 36 months if there has been no other discipline imposed during the past 36 months. ; Paper: Retain hard copy until it has been scanned into EIR

series_title: Application for Employment Bid Folders (Applicant Jackets)
series_description: Application for Employment Bid Folders contain all records collected and generated as part of the Department''s hiring and pre-employment processes including (as applicable to the position): applications/resumes and supporting documents, e.g., educational transcripts/diplomas, military discharge, and character/professional references; pre-employment questionnaires; criminal history checks; backgrounds and background checks; polygraph reports; applicant waivers for release of information; medical records, e.g. physician reports, medical histories, and medical screenings; completed pre-employment screening tools/reports; recruiting materials; position postings/ letters of inquiry; interview materials; certification lists; and hiring recommendation. If an applicant is rejected for hire, all records remain in the "Application for Employment Bid Folder." Records for rejected DPS - OSHP/OIU applicants are forwarded to OSHP Central Records. Records for other DPS rejected applicants are retained by DPS/HRM. Records shall be retained in accordance with the retention period established in this schedule and may be transferred to, and retained in, the ODPS Record Center. If an applicant is approved for hire, his/her application/resume and supporting documents are retained in HRM in accordance with "Employee Personnel File" SAN 760-1288. Backgrounds and background checks, criminal history checks, and polygraphs conducted for employment, are retained separately in accordance with "Backgrounds (background checks) for Employment" SAN 760-0788 and "Polygraphs for Employment" SAN 760-0773; and medical records for OSHP applicants collected during pre-employment are retained in the OSHP Health and Wellness section, in accordance with "OSHP Medical Records" SAN 760-1443. Note: This record retention schedule also applies to OSHP auxiliary member applicant records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain seven years from the date the applicant is rejected or the position is filled.

series_description: Contains all documents related to the completion of polygraph examinations conducted for department or external agency pre-employment processing including the release and waiver (HP24G), polygraph results, associated reports, audios or video files, and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in OSHP Central Records for seven years from date of exam. ; Electronic: Retain in network directory attached to OSHP Central Records for seven years from date of exam.

series_title: OSHA Incident Reports, Logs, and Annual Summaries
series_description: This series contains reports of incidents (OSHA 301), OSHA Logs (OSHA 300), and annual summaries related to employee work-related illnesses, injuries, and deaths. Records are entered and maintained in the ODPS HR/PeopleSoft system.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain current, plus five years in HR/PeopleSoft and ERIS systems. Reports relating to or indicating employee exposure to toxic or harmful substances or physical agents shall be retained 30 years from date of employee''s separation.

series_description: This record series contains: 1) Notarized charge of discrimination; 2) Agency response to complaint; 3) Correspondence between ODPS/EEO Unit and enforcement agency (OCRC, EEOC, and/or DAS/EOD); and 4) other correspondence and supporting documentation collected during case investigation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus seven years from date of final resolution. Current ecords are retained in HR/EEO office files and transferred to ODPS Record Center for retention.

series_description: This record series contains: 1) Employee request for reasonable accommodation; 2) Medical documentation; 3) Correspondence between ODPS/EEO Unit and employee; and 4) ODPS determination.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus seven years from date of final resolution. Current ecords are retained in HR/EEO office files and transferred to ODPS Record Center for retention.

series_description: This record series contains lists of appointments involving placement into advanced payroll steps.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus five years from date of hire. Current records are retained in HR/EEO office files and transferred to ODSP Record Center for retention.

series_description: This series contains records related to grievances between the agency and employees or the union and the procedures used to resolve them. Grievance case files contain: 1) initial grievance forms and supporting documentation; 2) step (2-3) responses; and 3) correspondence between agency and employees and/or union representatives. Grievances that enter arbitration will also contain files related to the arbitration proceedings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two years after date the case is closed and all remedies pursuant to the applicable labor agreement have been exhausted. Records are retained in HRM Labor Relations Office files. Record may be imaged; if imaged, paper may be destroyed when electronic record is validated. ; Electronic: Retain for two years after date the case is closed and all remedies pursuant to the applicable labor agreement have been exhausted. Records are retained in HRM Labor Relations network directory.

series_description: This record series contains source material, research, and final, original work products, e.g., work-sample and scenario exercises, questionnaires, and exams developed for the purpose of conducting pre-employment screenings and assessing applicant or employee competencies and performance. NOTE: Results of completed assessments are retained separately in accordance with Employee Personnel File (SAN 760-1288), Application for Employment Bid Folder (SAN 760-1492), or Promotional Exams and Assessments (SAN 760-0831), as applicable.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in OSHP Office of Personnel until superseded or obsolete. ; Electronic: Retain in OSHP Office of Personnel until superseded or obsolete.

series_description: This record series contains: 1) probationary, ad-hoc (special), and annual performance evaluations; 2) employee commendations; 3) employee performance improvement plans including performance goals; and 4) career development plans. NOTE: OSHP auxiliary member evaluation and development records are retained in the Office of Strategic Services in accordance with this schedule.
retention_statement: Paper: This medium applies to only OSHP auxiliary member evaluations. Retain current, plus seven years. ; Electronic: Retain current, plus seven years. Records retained in PeopleSoft and E-Performance System.

series_description: This series contains records related to exam and assessment development, preparation, and results for OSHP and OIU sworn employees. Records include candidate readiness interviews, commander evaluations, completed work exercises, test scores and evaluations, assessor recommendations for follow-up action, credit requests, CEC final reports, and supporting documentation, e.g. sign-in sheets, non-disclosure agreements, and correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain seven years following date of evaluation/ assessment in OSHP Office of Personnel Assessment Center files. These records may be digitized or transferred to the DPS Record Center during retention. ; Electronic: Retain seven (7) years following date of evaluation / assessment in OSHP Office of Personnel Assessment Center files

series_title: Apportioned and Non Apportioned Company/Organizational Logo Plates
series_description: This record series includes all company / organizational logo applications, petition packets, and artwork from company and organization representatives. This series will also include applicable statistical reports, sample plates, the coordinating action memo containing the plate/logo approval, documents and communications received during the new, revision, and reinstatement plate process.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for twenty-five (25) years from date of application. Records may be transferred to ODPS Record Center. ; Electronic: Correspondence received via email shall be printed. Digital artwork received via email or other electronic medium shall be saved and retained electronically on the office network. Retain for twenty-five years from date of application. Retain original emails in Microsoft Outlook for one year after digital files are saved and correspondence is printed.

series_description: The Wireless Communications Request (DPS 0077) is used to change or request a wireless communication device or devices, e.g. air card, flip phone, Blackberry, iPhone, iPad, iPad Mini, etc., to be used for Ohio Department of Public Safety business.
retention_statement: Paper: Current, plus one year ; Electronic: Current, plus one year

series_description: To document and record all Criminal Polygraphs Examinations conducted for OSHP and Outside Law Enforcement Agencies. (HP-24G, Audio/Video, Associated Reports, Graphs, Notes, etc.)
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain Audio/Video CD media in OSHP Central Records for one hundred (100) years. ; Paper: Retain hard copies in OSHP Central Records for one hundred (100) years.

series_description: This record series contains:1) Affirmative Action/Minority Business Enterprise Plans - Projected agency expenditures with minority business vendors; 2) Affirmative Action Plans - Analysis of agency employment practices, quantitatives, goals and objectives; 3) Equal Opportunity Minority Business Enterprise Statistical Reports - Quarterly reporting of expenditures with minority vendors; 4) Employee Demographic and Upward Mobility Reports - Report of total employee workforce by race, gender, and EEO job categories; 5) EEO Applicant Data Sheets - Race, gender, disability, and verteran status of applicants for employment; 6) EEO Exit Questionnaire Form - Voluntary questionnaire completed by exiting employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus seven years. Current records are retained in HR/EEO office files and transferred to ODPS Record Center for retention.

series_title: State registry of habitual OVI/OMWI offenders
series_description: This record series includes registry records created and maintained in accordance with section 5502.10 of the Revised Code, source documents received from the courts of conviction and related inquiries.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain source documents and related inquiries in office files for one year, plus current. ; Electronic: Retain source documents and related inquiries in Microsoft Outlook or office network for one year, plus curent. In accordance with paragraph (C) of section 5502.10, registry records are automatically purged and refreshed every thirty days via established data management processes to ensure that the registry contains accurate and current information.

series_description: This series contains records of employment for each ODPS employee including: documents related to hire (application/resume and supporting documents, e.g., educational transcripts/diplomas, military discharge, character/professional references, and memo of approval); employee work rule agreements and acknowledgements; personnel forms, which may include, but are not limited to, ID badge information, requests for specialty positions, applications/approvals of secondary employment, Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) and essential employee designations, changes of address, and PERS form A; completion of service letters; personnel actions (not including disciplinary actions with the exception of termination); letters of commendation and recognition; emergency contact information; and exit interviews. Note: The following personnel records are retained separately: Backgrounds (background checks) and polygraphs conducted for employment in accordance with SAN 760-0788 and SAN 760-0773; Employee medical and benefits records in accordance with SAN 760-1443 (non-OSHP/OIU employees) and SAN 760-1072 (OSHP/OIU employees); Employee training records in accordance with SAN 760-1538; Employee performance evaluations in accordance with SAN 760-1437; I-9 Employee Verification Forms in accordance with SAN 760-1532; and Employee disciplinary records in accordance with SAN 760-0787.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for life of service, plus 50 years from employee''''''''s date of separation. Record may be retained electronically. If record is imaged and retained electronically, paper may be destroyed as soon as scan is confirmed and validated. ; Electronic: Personnel Actions are retained in the Electronic Personnel Action Request (EPAR) system. Remaining records, if retained electronically, are retained in HR''''''''s Intellinetics System, PeopleSoft, and ERIS. Retain for life of service, plus 50 years from employee''''''''s date of separation.

series_title: Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSHP) Auxiliary Unit Member Files
series_description: This record series contains auxiliary member records of service with OSHP and includes: applications/resumes and supporting documentation; liability release waivers; member commissions and auxiliary general orders; merit and service awards, commendations, and letters of recognition; documentation of any violations of Auxiliary General Orders or Division policy; physical fitness evaluations; record of issued uniforms and equipment and other miscellaneous documents, e.g. emergency contact information. Note: The following auxiliary member records are retained separately: Pre-service backgrounds in accordance with SAN 760-0788; pre-service polygraphs are retained in accordance with 760-0773; weapon familiarization in accordance with SAN 760-1480; and training records in accordance with SAN 760-1538.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for entire period of service plus two (2) years. ; Paper: Retain for entire period of service plus two (2) years.

series_description: To provide an accurate record of supplies dispensed from patrol storage facilities directly into patrol vehicles and when making credit purchases of petroleum products for patrol-owned vehicles, as well as agency-owned vehicles. Form: HP-49, HP-49A
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus three years and until audited and report is released.

series_title: Motorcycle Ohio Program skill waiver and course completion cards
series_description: The Motorcycle Ohio Program issues each student a completion card upon the successful completion of the Basic Rider Course (BRC) or the Basic Rider Course Returning Rider class (BRC-RR). The course completion card (MOP0014) is issued with a control number and verified in the BASS system by the BMV and enables a student to bypass the skills exam and obtain a motorcycle endorsement.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years ; Electronic: 5 years

series_description: CONSISTS OF ALL RECORDS WHICH DOCUMENT, OR ARE THE BASIS FOR, PAYROLL INCLUDING TIME REPORTS, ACTIVITY SHEETS, TIME CARDS, OVERTIME/COMPTIME/FLEXTIME AUTHORIZATIONS, LEAVE REQUESTS, and UNION LEAVE. THIS SCHEDULE IS TO BE USED BY ALL DIVISIONS OF THE OHIO DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until associated payroll records are audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies resolved. ; Electronic: Retained in HMS until associated payroll records are audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies resolved.

series_title: International Registration Plan (IRP) Customer and Permit Agency Accounts
series_description: This record series contains accounts of individual customers (lessors or owners of apportionable vehicles), annual registrations and accounts of permit agencies and agents authorized to process and issue temporary credentials (72-hour Trip Permits) under the International Registration Plan (IRP), notifications for the registrations/license plates of all commercial motor vehicles assigned to a registrant or carrier with a shared USDOT#, and this record series includes: initial/renewal applications (OH-F5, Ohio IRP Application; BMV 4200, Permit Agency Application for 72-hour Trip Permits; BMV 4867, Notifcation of Revocation of Commercial Motor Vehicle Registrations/License Plates) and supporting documentation; registration fee invoices; trip permit agreements; trip permit agency weekly sales reports (BMV 4201); records of sold or voided trip permits; and miscellaneous correspondence. These records are subject to state and federal audits.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current registration year, plus three years, and until both state and federal audits (if required) are reported and released. Record may be imaged. If imaged, paper record may be destroyed, once the electronic record is validated. ; Electronic: Retain current registration year, plus three years, and until both state and federal audits (if required) are reported and released. These records are retained in the BMV/IRP section''s shared drive files.

series_title: Request for Abstract of Driving Record
series_description: Record may consist of OBMV Record Request form (BMV 1173), Reply to Request for BMV Records form (BMV 1107), Abstract Request form (BMV 1145), BMV Record Search form (BMV 2433), Driver License Verification Letter form (BMV 2505), Record Request Denial (BMV 3341), Driver License Application form (BMV 2002), Vehicle Registration Record or Title Record form (BMV 1149), and copy of receipt of payment for BMV records. The forms may contain the person''s full name, address, city, state, zip code, date of birth, social security number, driver license number, vehicle identification number, license plate number, and title number.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned into CPIC database and verified ; Electronic: Retain 3 years from date added to CPIC database

series_description: Record consists of the Donor Registry Enrollment form (BMV 3346) or comparable documents provided by Organ Procurement Organizations (OPO). The forms may contain the person''s full name, address, city, state, zip code, date of birth, social security number, and driver license number.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned into CPIC database and verified ; Electronic: Retain in CPIC database until fulfilled or revoked

series_description: Employee analysis/plan of actions, as well as, findings, recommendations, and correspondence by the early intervention review committee.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus two years in the State Highway Patrol Office of Personnel.

series_description: Applicant field records (Form HP-47F) document initial contacts with potential applicants for positions as trooopers or police officers with the Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSHP). Applicant field records contain information for each potential applicant including name, address, and source codes that represent where and how the contact was made. Records are retained at the OSHP Academy Recruitment Office.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus two years from date contact is made and information is collected.

series_title: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) Card Summary Report
series_description: This report is used by the Ohio Investigative Unit's Food Stamp Agents and is submitted to the Food Stamp Administrator on a monthly basis to track all transactions occurring on covert Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) cards during the month. The Food Stamp Administrator checks the report against Job and Family Services’ Electronic Payment Processing Information Control (EPPIC) program as verification that all information entered by the Agents is correct. Once the information is verified, the report is filed for future reference.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in Central Ohio files for five (5) years or until audited by the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) or its designee and the report is released. ; Electronic: Retain in Ohio Agent Secure Information System database files for five (5) years or until audited by the SNAP or its designee and the report is released.

series_title: Move, Add, Change, and Equipment Requests
series_description: The Move, Add, Change (MAC) and Equipment request form (DPS 0200) is used to provide supervisors and MAC administrators with procedures and checklists for account creation, access to data systems, equipment, and workspace set-up. This record series may include, but is not limited to, the following forms used to request computer hardware and/or software, remote access, portable computing device(s), cell phone(s), smartphones(s), and air card(s): DPS 0058 - Application for Remote Access; DPS 0060 - Data Services Request; DPS 0077 - Wireless Communications Request; DPS 0200 - MAC and Equipment Request; and DPS 0222 - Request for Portable Computing Device.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until electronically entered into tracking system ; Electronic: One year and 30 days.

series_description: All financial and programmatic records related to the receipt and expenditure of federal grant funding including, but not limited to, grant applications and supporting documentation; award and acceptance forms; financial reports including budgets, cash requests, expenditures, and source documentation; records of purchase and/or acquisiton; project information; use agreements; and relevant audit reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current year plus three years from the date of the federal "Final Financial Report" for the applicable grant and until the State Single Audit has been completed. ; Electronic: Retain current year plus three years from the date of the federal "Final Financial Report" for the applicable grant and until the State Single Audit has been completed.

series_description: This series contains Ohio Investigative Unit agents’ application materials for covert IDs and all other identification materials. During employment and upon separation, the materials should be kept separate from an agent’s personnel file to maintain confidentiality.
retention_statement: Paper: Reports are retained permanently in Ohio Agent Secure Information System (OASIS) database. ; Electronic: Reports are retained permanently in OASIS database.

series_description: This series consists of applications and certificates for Self-Insurance in accordance with sections 4509.72, 4509.59, and 4509.62 of the Revised Code. BMV 3148 – Application For Self-Insurance General BMV 3149 – Application For Self-Insurance Religious Organization BMV 3140 – Certificate of Self-Insurance BMV 3146 - Application for Certificate Deposit of $30,000 in Money or Government Bonds BMV 2182 - Certificate of Financial Responsibility, R.C. 4509.62 BMV 2180 - Application – Certificate of Financial Responsibility BMV 2181 - Certificate of Financial Responsibility, R.C. 4509.59
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in BMV Compliance Unit office files for current registration period (five years), plus one year following date of expiration.

series_description: This record series contains evidence of employees’ internal (conducted by the Department’s Workforce Planning and Development section) and external (professional development, Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSHP) academy classes, OSHP roll call, specialized law enforcement/peace officer license and certification etc…) training. NOTES: 1) OSHP auxiliary member training records are retained in accordance with this schedule. 2) OSHP breathalyzer (blood content or BAC) and speed measuring device (SMD) certifications are retained in accordance with SANs 760-1531 and 760-1530.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for life of employment, plus seven (7) years. Records are retained in component office files in the Department’s Workforce Planning and Development section and OSHP Academy, as well Ohio Homeland Security (OHS), Ohio Investigative Unit (OIU), Emergency Management Agency, and OSHP district offices. Records may be imaged. If imaged, paper may be destroyed when electronic record is validated. Records may be transferred to DPS Record Center during retention. ; Electronic: Retain for life of employment, plus seven (7) years. These records are retained in several DPS database systems: ERIS, DPS Training Campus, OSHP Academy’s network directory, OHS Training System, OSHP’s Information Portal, and network attached storage devices (Microsoft Environment).

series_description: This record series contains the course curriculum, syllabus, and source documents, e.g., lesson plans, course objectives, assignments, exams, handouts, visual training aids, course attendance roster, and course evaluations, for trainings internally developed and/or presented to ODPS employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office component files until course is superseded or obsolete. ; Electronic: Retain in office component files until course is superseded or obsolete.

series_description: The Synar program is a federally funded tobacco compliance program. Confidential informants (CIs), who are under the age of 21, are sent into stores to try to purchase tobacco products. If products are sold to the CI, the seller is cited for selling tobacco products to a person under age 21. Synar program files (compliance checks) include: CI Statement (INV0045), CI Payment Ledger (INV0029), Evidence Custody Document (INV0011), Arrest Report (INV8025), Memo Request for Compliance Check, and Cost Sheet (INV0063).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years or until audited and the audit report is released unless required to retain by a specific agreement. ; Electronic: Retain in the Ohio Agent Secure Information System (OASIS) for 5 years or until audited and the audit report is released unless required to retain longer by a specific agreement.

series_description: Series covers DPS postings to Social Media sites including, but not limited to Facebook, Twitter, Youtube, etc. This information includes, but is not limited to press releases, videos, events, and general information about DPS. The series also covers non-DPS generated postings removed by DPS pursuant to DPS Policy 401.01. Information posted that falls under a separate retention schedule will be kept according to that retention schedule.
retention_statement: Electronic: DPS postings - Retain until updated or no longer needed Non-DPS Generated postings - Retain for current month

series_title: Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSHP) Employee Medical Records
series_description: This series contains medical records related to all OSHP troopers, which may include, but is not limited to, medical histories; medical test results; informed consent/release forms; health screening questionnaires; medical, psychological, and physical fitness evaluations; physical fitness plans; medical referrals; medical records from external sources; medication lists; medical deferral requests; and supporting documentation. This series also contains medical histories for OSHP civilian employees through 2012. Note: Beginning in 2013, medical histories for OSHP civilian employees are retained in accordance with SAN 760-1443, "Employee Benefits and Medical History Files" SAN 760-1443.
retention_statement: Paper: Records are to be retained current, plus 50 years from date of separation. Records related to OSHP civilian employees are to be retained current, plus 7 years from date of separation. Records may be transferred to ODPS Record Center for retention. Record may be retained electronically in HR Intellinetics System. If record is imaged and retained electronically, paper may be destroyed as soon as scan is confirmed and validated. ; Electronic: If retained electronically, records are retained in HR''s Intellinetics System. Records related to OSHP troopers are to be retained current, plus 50 years from date of separation. Records related to OSHP civilians are to be retained current, plus 7 years from date of separation.

series_title: Employee Benefits and Medical History Files
series_description: This series contains records related to all Department of Public Safety (DPS) employee benefits, which include documentation of employee enrollment in the health, dental, and vision programs; FMLA benefits and all supporting documentation; "Wellness Facility Releases of Liability" DPS form 0008; applications for disability benefits and all supporting documentation; and fitness for duty reports. This series also contains records related to DPS employee medical histories, with the exception of Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSHP) troopers, whose medical histories are retained at the OSHP Academy in accordance with SAN 760-1072 (Ohio State Highway Patrol Employee Medical Records). Note: Medical histories for OSHP civilian employees through 2012 are also retained at the OSHP Academy in accordance with SAN 760-1072.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus seven years from date of separation in HR/Benefits and Medical Office files. Record may be imaged. If record is imaged and retained electronically, paper may be destroyed as soon as scan is confirmed and validated. ; Electronic: Retain current, plus seven years from date of separation in HR/Intellinetics System.

series_title: Automated Title Processing System (ATPS) - Miscellaneous Transactions
series_description: This record series includes monetary receipts created during completion of miscellaneous transactions that do not relate to the issuance of a certificate of title, e.g., fishing licenses, copies of records, reports, affidavits, and passports.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in the Automated Title Processing System (ATPS) for 7 years from the date of transaction.

series_description: Facility Services Project Files contain information regarding major/minor architectural, mechanical, electrical and/or plumbing changes to the Shipley building, Centre School, Alum Creek, and EMA facilities of the Ohio Department of Public Safety (ODPS). The project files may also contain information regarding office renovations/builds/additions, etc. The project files include, but are not limited to the following documentation: proposal; internal/external correspondence (e.g. electronic mail, faxes, U.S. mail); ODPS work orders; vendor work orders; requisitions; electrical/mechanical permits; Authorization to Purchase memos (DPS 0135); purchase order notifications; purchase orders; invoices; packing lists; drawings; project specifications; ISTVs; and Feasibility Study, which contains information regarding: space criteria; impact on a section''''''''s operation during construction; estimated lead time to schedule project; approximate length of construction time to complete project; necessary resources; and cost estimate.
retention_statement: Paper: Architectural, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing project files retained permanently. All other projects retained for 5 years after completion and/or after warranties expire. ; Electronic: Architectural, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing project files retained permanently. All other projects retained for 5 years after completion and/or after warranties expire.

series_description: This record series contains: initial and renewal applications and supporting documentation, e.g., laboratory analyses and evidence of liability insurance, submitted by manufacturers of ignition interlock devices for licensure (licensee) and certification of their devices; their annual reports of sales and net profits of their devices and supporting documentation; copies of manufacturer licenses and device certificates issued to the licensee; and all correspondence between licensee and the Department.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus 4 years following date of lapse, expiration, or termination of license/certification. Records may be transferred to DPS Record Center during retention period. Records may be imaged. If imaged, paper may be destroyed when electronic record is validated. ; Electronic: Retain current, plus 4 years following date of lapse, expiration, or termination of license/certification. Records are retained on network drives in the Ohio Traffic Safety Office.

series_title: Ignition Interlock Device (IID) Licensee Investigation/Disciplinary Files
series_description: In accordance with rule 4501-45-05 of the Ohio Administrative Code, the Director of the Department of Public Safety may deny an application for licensure or certification filed by an IID manufacturer and may take disciplinary action against such license or certification. This record series contains all documents gathered as part of the administrative action process including: investigation into alleged violations; reports of investigations; recommendations for disciplinary action; and adjudication results.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain current, plus 4 years after case is closed and all administrative remedies have been exhausted. Records are retained on network drives in the Ohio Traffic Safety Office. ; Paper: Retain current, plus 4 years after case is closed and all administrative remedies have been exhausted. Records may be transferred to DPS Record Center during retention period. Records may be imaged. If imaged, paper may be destroyed when electronic record is validated.

series_description: Contains current postings and previously removed postings on the Department of Public Safety''s website. Postings include list of approved IID Licensees (contact information and certified devices), IID installation locations, and any other postings/materials as needed.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain current, plus 6 months following date of removal. Records are retained on network drives in the Ohio Traffic Safety Office.

series_description: This record series contains documents created and received during the administrative hearing process in compliance with Chapter 119. of the Revised Code. Files may include notices of opportunity for hearing, hearing requests, hearing scheduling letters, hearing examiner reports of recommendation, final orders, and other correspondence and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until all administrative appeals have been exhausted, plus one year. ; Electronic: Retain until all administrative appeals have been exhausted, plus one year.

series_description: Includes reference and research material used to draft rules, transcripts of public hearings, and public comments/agency responses regarding proposed rules.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year after rule is final-filed. ; Electronic: Retain for one year after rule is final-filed.

series_title: Request for Records Retention Schedule (Form DPS129)
series_description: Ohio Department of Public Safety form DPS129 is used by agency records management coordinators to request a new records retention schedule, revise an existing schedule, or request that an existing schedule be made obsolete. This record series includes the form with required routing and approval signatures signifying that the schedule has been approved for submission to the Department of Administrative Services, as well as any supporting documentation, e.g., records analyses.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the office of Distribution and Inventory Services for two years following the date of approved schedule''s publication to DAS'' Records Information Management System (RIMS). ; Electronic: Retain in the office of Distribution and Inventory Services for two years following the date of approved schedule''s publication to DAS'' Records Information Management System (RIMS).

series_description: The Ohio State Highway Patrol''s (OSHP) Skywatch Tower video recording equipment is used by Ohio law enforcement agencies to support security efforts during events within the agencies'' respective jurisdictions. In accordance with OSHP''s Skywatch Tower deployment agreement, all agencies that use Skywatch Tower equipment are responsible for downloading video captured during deployment and retaining the video per their respective agencies'' retention policies. Upon completion of each deployment, OSHP technology communication technicians shall immediately delete all video captured during the event from the Skywatch Tower equipment hard-drives, which will be immediately reformatted and readied for the next deployment. OSHP Technology Communications Services will not retain any video prior to deleting and reformatting. If OSHP or the Department of Public Safety''s Ohio Investigative Unit uses the Skywatch Tower equipment, downloaded video that is routine will be retained in accordance with schedule 760-0826 (Audio and Video Recordings) for thirty days, while downloaded video that leads to investigative action will be retained with the investigation in accordance with schedule 760-0431 (Criminal Investigations).
retention_statement: Electronic: At the end of each deployment, Skywatch Tower video captures will be immediately deleted from Skywatch Tower video equipment hard-drives, and the hard-drives will be reformatted for the next deployment.

series_description: Public records requests are requests that the agency receives from any person to inspect, review, or obtain copies of the agency''s existing records that are not accompanied by authorization/consent forms. This record series contains initial requests for existing records, the Department''s letter of response, imaged copies of records provided during response, all correspondence between requester and the Department during the fulfillment of the request (if any), and the transaction histories of these requests retained in the Department''s Public Records Database. NOTE: Subpoenas and requests for discovery are not subject to this schedule.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus one year from date request is closed. ; Electronic: Retain current, plus one year from date request is closed.

series_description: The Application for Remote Access (form DPS 0058) is used to provide supervisors the ability to request access for remote connectivity.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until electronically entered into tracking system and validated ; Electronic: One year and 30 days.

series_description: This record series includes all vehicle registration applications (initial and renewal), as well as supplementary forms, and supporting documentation created and received during the registration process completed online or in person at a deputy registrar.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus three years from date of registration''s expiration. Record may be imaged. If imaged, retain until validated and authenticated ; Electronic: Retain current, plus three years from date of registration''s expiration. These records are retained in the BMV''''''''s driver license system.

series_title: Administrative hearing records for driver license suspension cases
series_description: This record series contains Bureau of Motor Vehicle (BMV) adjudication/administrative orders related to driver license suspension cases and records of appeal that are filed with the courts of common pleas pursuant to Chapter 119. of the Revised Code.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the Office of Legal Services. Retain adjudication/administrative orders for one year from date of issuance and records of appeal for one year following date on which all appeals have run. ; Electronic: Retain in the Office of Legal Services. Retain adjudication/administrative orders for one year from date of issuance and records of appeal for one year following date on which all appeals have run.

series_description: Prevailing wage documents are vendor payroll records of wages paid to contract employees who work on a capital project and/or public improvement project. These records include all disbursements of wages paid by contracted employees who are required to receive not less than the prevailing rate of wages.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain documents from commencement of capital projects and/or public improvement projects for one year after completion and until successful completion of warranty inspection

series_description: This record series contains information related to the preparation and completion of a biennial audit of Ohio State Patrol’s (OSP) criminal intelligence function. Records may include audit checklists, reports of evaluation and recommended actions, documentation of submission to system in compliance with project criteria pursuant to
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in Intelligence Unit’s data-system for two years, provided that the biennial information audit is complete and reported.

series_description: This record series contains information and supporting documentation regarding reasonable suspicion of criminal conduct or activity submitted to the Ohio State Patrol (OSP) Intelligence Unit. Records include source and method of receipt of information; documented assessment of relevancy in accordance with
retention_statement: Electronic: Records are required to meet submission criteria for criminal intelligence information (i.e., "validated") as set forth in 28 CFR 23 prior to retention. Validated records shall be retained in the Intel Unit’s data-system for no more than five years. Pursuant to 28 CFR 23, records shall be periodically reviewed (i.e., "purge review") during retention for continued compliance to 28 CFR 23 submission criteria. If a record is reviewed and validated as continuing to meet submission criteria, it will receive a new maximum retention period of five years. Records found to no longer meet the criteria shall be purged immediately.

series_title: Ohio State Patrol (OSP) Intelligence Products Reference Library
series_description: Information, reports, and research results collected from various law enforcement databases and retained for the purpose of trending and analysis.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in Intelligence Unit’s data-system until no longer relevant or of administrative value.

series_title: Ohio State Patrol (OSP) Intelligence Unit Working Files
series_description: This record series contains notes, research material, and supplementary resources used in the analysis of field reports and requests for service received by the OSP Intelligence Unit.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in the network directory for one year.

series_title: Ohio State Patrol (OSP) Requests for Service
series_description: This record series contains requests for assistance with investigations, assessments, and event planning that are submitted to the OSP Intelligence Unit. Records include source and method of receipt of requests; the final work product and/or response, e.g., bulletins, profiles, and case reports; and as applicable, dissemination to appropriate law enforcement and/or public safety agencies. Note: If any request is evaluated and found to meet the threshold of reasonable suspicion of criminal activity set forth in
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in Intelligence Unit’s data-system for ten years and destroy provided that the information is no longer relevant as a security threat.

series_title: Law Enforcement Requests for Actions to be Taken on a Vehicle Title
series_description: Record series to include documents submitted by Law Enforcement / BMV Investigators requesting action to be taken on a vehicle title. Requests/actions may include, but are not limited to, title cancellations for open investigations regarding vehicle ownership/title transfer fraud (e.g. fraudulent documents, signatures, forms of payment/bad checks, etc.); administrative holds that Title Support employees place on a VIN in ATPS to prevent future title transfers to occur for that vehicle within the state of Ohio; administrative holds that Title Support employees remove from a VIN in ATPS to allow for future title transfers to occur; authority letters that instruct Clerk of Courts Title offices to take some type of action on a title (e.g. issue a title, remove a lien, etc.); order in letters that inform individuals their title has been cancelled and also instructs them to immediately mail that title to the BMV. This series also includes any supporting documentation for the above mentioned requests/actions.
retention_statement: Electronic: 7 years after the case is closed or no longer than 20 years if case remains open/unresolved. ; Paper: 7 years after the date the case is closed, and in the event the case remains open/unresolved, records shall be retained no longer than 20 years. Records may be imaged.

series_description: This record series includes the registration application (BMV 4349), the reporting form (BMV 4382), and all temporary tag information entered into the dealer system.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years from the date of section transfer to ODPS Record Center. ; Electronic: Retain reported temporary tags in the BMV dealer system for three years from expiration date. Temporary tags issued to a dealer with no record (not reported) retain in the BMV dealer system for three years from the issue date of inventory .

series_title: Motor Vehicle Dealer and Salvage Board Cases and New Dealer Franchise Protests.
series_description: This record series includes applications, criminal records, court documents, photographs, etc. that are used as evidence in an administrative hearing or protest.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records for five years from the date the case is closed. ; Electronic: Retain records for five years from the date the case is closed.

series_title: Petitions for Organizational Special License Plate
series_description: This record series includes all petitions received for the implementation or revision of a special license plate.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years from the date of submission

series_title: Certificates of Title Records in the Automated Title Processing System (ATPS)
series_description: Each certificate of title record contains data entered by the Clerk of Courts when issuing a title. This includes owner information (name, address, SSN/FEIN), previous owner information (name, address, SSN/FEIN), transfer on death beneficiary information (name, SSN/FEIN), vehicle or watercraft description (year, make, body type, model, vehicle identification number, watercraft identification number, length, horsepower), odometer reading, brand information, purchase price, trade-in credit, sales tax, user name of the issuing deputy clerk of courts, county of issuance, county of residence, title number, document control number, date of issuance, purchase date, lien information (name, address of lienholder and date of lien filing), date of birth if owner or beneficiary is a minor, evidence type surrendered when applying for title, previous title number, title status and inactivation reason, title status date, tax exemption code, title comments.
retention_statement: Paper: N/A ; Electronic: Current, active titles, plus 10 years from date of inactivation for motor vehicles, special vehicles and watercraft; and current, active titles, plus 30 years from date of activation for manufactured and mobile homes. These records are retained in the Automated Title Processing System (ATPS).

series_description: To provide a uniform method of recording traffic violations (HP-7) in a digital format in the eCitation system / Ohio Trooper Information System (OTIS) database.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retained in the OTIS / eCitation database for five years plus the current year and

series_title: Traffic Crash Statistics and eCitation Statistics
series_description: Traffic crash and eCitation statistics are compiled from traffic crash reports and electronic crash data, and eCitation received from Ohio law enforcement agencies following their investigations of traffic crashes, and traffic law enforcement. The crash data is collected and maintained pursuant to Revised Code section 5502.01, which directs the Ohio Department of Public Safety to compile, analyze, and publish motor vehicle accident statistics for the purpose of education and research. This record series includes the OH-1 (form HSY7001)and the eCitation. Collected OH-1 data is published to the Department of Public Safety''s Crash Statistics website at: https://ext.dps.state.oh.us/crashstatistics/CrashReports.aspx
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and validated ; Electronic: Retain current year, plus ten years.

series_title: Applications for Certification and Agreement for CDL Third Party Test sites / Examiners
series_description: This record series contains the forms utilized by the Ohio Department of Public Safety (ODPS), Bureau of Motor Vehicles to provide Commercial Driver License (CDL) instruction and certification through a Third Party administrator. FORMS: BMV 5811 - "Application for Certification of Commercial Driver License Third Party Tester" - This application allows the prospective Third Party to apply for a position as a CDL Test site. The CDL Tester must maintain this record for 2 years after closure of CDL test site. BMV 5812 - "Application for Certification of Commercial Driver License Third Party Examiner" - This application allows the prospective Third Party Tester to apply for a position as a CDL test examiner. The CDL tester must maintain this record for 2 years after the conclusion of the CDL testing cycle. BMV 5813 - "Agreement for the Administration of CDL Skills Test" - This is a contract agreement between ODPS and a Third Party (the "Contractor") to administer CDL skills tests in accordance with the provisions of Ohio Revised Code (ORC), Chapter 4506, as the ORC may be enacted or amended from time to time, or instructions provided through the ODPS, and regulations of the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration. BMV 4891 - "Application for Class-D Third Party testing site (Pilot)" - This application allows the prospective Third Party Tester to apply for a position as a Third Party Class D test site as allowed in
retention_statement: Paper: BMV 5811 - All records must be retained two years from the date of business closure of the CDL Third Party Test site.BMV 5812 - All records must be retained two years from the date after the Third Party Examiner leaves the employment of the Third Party Tester. BMV 5813 - All records must be retained two years from the date of execution of the contract. BMV 4891 - All records must be retained two years from the execution of the contract.

series_title: Contracts for goods, services, and property leases
series_description: The records retained in this series include all Contracts, Leases, and Major Acquisitions Unit records pertaining to the procurement of goods, services, supplies, equipment and real estate rentals for the Ohio Department of Public Safety and include: 1) A copy of the procurement request, request to purchase, or memoranda; 2) A copy of the signed contract or lease; 3) Copies of all bids, proposals, or quotes (unless maintained by DAS); 4) Release and permit authorization (if needed); 5) Controlling Board authorization (if needed); 6) Vendor complaint documentation (if applicable); 7) Facility Access approval forms; 8) Database summary sheet (if applicable); 9) Authorization to Purchase (if needed); 10) Review and Approval forms; 11) Signed Coordinating Action forms (if applicable); 12) The ITB, RFP, or RFQ solicitation document (if applicable); 13) A copy of the issued purchase order (if applicable); and 14) Other related documentation, correspondence, and support information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current plus 8 years from the date of expiration or cancellation Per Revised Code 2305.06 ; Electronic: Retain current plus 8 years from the date of expiration or cancellation Per Revised Code 2305.06

series_description: The Deaths in Custody Reporting form, CJS0014, is used to collect arrest-related death data from state and local agencies in Ohio that is used for the purpose of the Death in Custody Reporting Act of 2013.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years. ; Electronic: Retain for three years.

series_title: Motorcycle Ohio Program (MOP) class paperwork/ fiscal
series_description: MOP0010 (11/12): “Student Signature Rosters”; MOP0017 (11/12): “Basic Rider Course Skills Evaluation Score Sheets”; MOP0028 (02/05): “Motorcycle Ohio Student Report Form”; MOP0032 (12/10) “BRC/ERC Instructor Check off List”; MOP0036 (02/13): “Motorcycle Safety Course Waiver and Indemnification”; MOP0039 (11/12): Course Satisfaction Survey”; MOP0058 (01/09): “Instructor Noon-Crash Report”; MOP0062 (12/12): “BRC Individual Skill Evaluation Sheet”; MOP0065 (03/13): “Motorcycle Ohio Incident Report Form”; MOP0066 (03/13): “Waiver and Release for participants with Medical Condition”; Inactive Instructor files; Purchase orders; Contracts; Refunds; Reciprocities: Grantee invoices; Class paperwork; Federal grant invoices; Employees sign-in sheet and Phone logs.
retention_statement: Paper: Return current fiscal year plus two (2) years. ; Electronic: Return current fiscal year plus two (2) years.

series_description: This series contains: Special Operations Plan (OHP 0448), Daily Operations Order (OHP 0119), OIU Special Operations Plan (INV 0148), and All Hazards Plan – Field Operations Order (OHP 0117). This series documents the approval of planned actions involving surveillance details, execution of search warrants, undercover missions, high risk deployments, ongoing operations, and bait/arrest operations by Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSHP) personnel.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus 2 years following execution of operation in applicable section. ; Electronic: Retain current, plus 2 years following execution of operation in applicable section.

series_description: All financial and programmatic records related to the receipt and expenditure of state grant funding including, but not limited to, grant applications and supporting documentation; award and acceptance forms; financial reports including budgets, cash requests, expenditures, and source documentation; records of purchase and/or acquisition; project information; use agreements; and relevant audit reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years from closeout of the last applicant to the program. ; Electronic: Retain three years from closeout of the last applicant to the program.

series_title: Dealer Motor Vehicle Shows and Displays
series_description: This record series includes all documents that are received from dealers and sponsors requesting the display of motor vehicles by dealership(s), and any correspondence from the BMV.
retention_statement: Paper: Retained for three months from upload date. ; Electronic: Retain for two years from date of display/show.

series_title: Motor Vehicle Dealer License, Miscellaneous Business Plates, and Salvage License
series_description: This record series includes all paper and scanned applications and/or documents that are submitted for a motor vehicle dealers license, miscellaneous business plates, and salvage licenses that have been issued by the BMV dealer licensing section.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three months from scan date. ; Electronic: Retain records for seven years from the date of the dealer expiration or cancellation, unless the record is marked as a board or protest case.

series_description: This record series includes all paper and scanned applications and/or documents that are submitted for a salespersons license is issued by the BMV dealer licensing section.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three months from scan date. ; Electronic: Retain for two years from the date of cancellation or inactive date.

series_title: Dealer System Computer Generated Reports and Files
series_description: This record series includes computer generated reports and files from the Dealer System on a daily, weekly, or quarterly basis(dealer permits, correction letters, salespersons license, etc...)
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in the Dealer System for 30 days from date of generation.

series_description: This record series includes all fingerprint cards that have been on file with the dealer licensing section and electronic fingerprint records that have been transmitted to the dealer system from BCI & I.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain fingerprint cards for one (1) year from the date the fingerprints were taken. ; Electronic: Retain fingerprint cards for one (1) year from the date the fingerprints were taken.

series_description: This record series includes all temporary tag (tags)request forms that are submitted by dealerships for ordering tag/sticker inventories and includes receipt number(s) and number of tags/stickers ordered.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for a fiscal year and fiscal report is completed and all discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Retain for a fiscal year and fiscal report is completed and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: To provide a record of evidence and recovered property collected, including its chain of custody, and to provide a method for requesting laboratory examination of the property (OHP 0161/HP-28, Property Control Form). To provide a means to review each lab case for technical accuracy (OHP 0079, Technical Review Checklist). To provide a means to record testing of controlled substances by lab personnel (OHP 0084, Controlled Substance Worksheet).
retention_statement: Electronic: HP-28s that are part of other reports such as OH-1s and case investigations assume the retention period of those reports. HP-28s that are part of a death investigation are retained permanently. Copies of all referenced reports are retained by the laboratory for nine (9) years plus current ; Paper: HP-28s that are part of other reports such as OH-1s and case investigations assume the retention period of those reports. HP-28s that are part of a death investigation are retained permanently. Copies of all referenced reports are retained by the laboratory for nine (9) years plus current

series_description: This record series includes Vehicle Registration General Correspondence provided to customers upon request and/or qualification, including but not limited to, template letters, memos, etc..., for informative purposes only.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain an electronic reference of applicable identifiers for a period of six months from the date of correspondence issuance/creation.

series_description: When State Highway Patrol investigates a crime or incident, the Division will complete various forms for obtaining information during the response. At that time a case is established in the OTIS reporting system database. OSHP reports may contain the following documents: HP-24, HP-24F, HP-24K, HP-24L, HP-24R, HP-24S, HP-24T, HP-25, HP-25C, HP-25D, HP-28, HP-60, HP-70A, photographs/digital images, audio and/or video recordings, HP-12, HP-58A, BMV-3608, BMV 2431,HP-40B, etc.)
retention_statement: Microform: Retain permanently.Referred to in future and ongoing investigations. There is no specific ORC section requiring this retention, however these are OSHP case files which continue to be useful for public records requests, background investigations, historical purposes, and general documentation of criminal activity/statistics. The OSHP often receive requests for old reports from the public, attorneys, universities, other governmental and non-profit agencies, and from our own investigators and Statistical Analysis Unit. ; Paper: Retain on hard copy until scanned and indexed into the document storage system. Once the hard copy has been scanned and indexed the hardcopy can be destroyed. Investigation reports that involve capital crimes or are unique in nature shall also be retained in hard copy form permanently ; Electronic: Retain permanently. Cases on microfilm must be scanned and indexed into the document storage system before microfilm is destroyed.Referred to in future and ongoing investigations. There is no specific ORC section requiring this retention, however these are OSHP case files which continue to be useful for public records requests, background investigations, historical purposes, and general documentation of criminal activity/statistics. The OSHP often receive requests for old reports from the public, attorneys, universities, other governmental and non-profit agencies, and from our own investigators and Statistical Analysis Unit.
disposition: Microform: Permanent ; Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Permanent

series_description: To record and track the log in and log out of visitors to certain restricted areas within the Ohio State Highway Patrol(e.g., Central Records, GHQ Facility, Crime Lab, Intelligence Unit, etc.) on a Visitor Log form, OHP0151.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year plus current ; Electronic: Retain for one year plus current

series_title: Telephone Logs, Bills, Call Records, and Attendance Sheets
series_description: This record includes listings of incoming, outgoing, and long distance calls made to/from the Dealer Licensing Section and employee attendance (sign-in)sheets for each work day.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for six (6) months

series_description: This record includes, but not limited to, documents of on-going projects, issues, total number of dealer permits/registrations, total renewals generated, and temporary tags sales, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until electronic version of the report is created ; Electronic: Retain reports for one (1) fiscal year

series_description: Used to document the review of dispatcher professional operations to determine any irregularities. Includes OHP 0048.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until biennial dispatch center audits are completed, as applicable. ; Electronic: Retain until biennial dispatch center audits are completed, as applicable.

series_description: To comply with the Ohio Revised Code 3937.41(D) prohibiting unfavorable treatment of law enforcement officers by automobile insurance companies as the result of traffic crashes occurring while on official duty and operating a division-owned or leased vehicle (HP-3C).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until the affected officer''s driving record has been updated with an "L1" indicator. Destroy after verification or one calendar year.. ; Electronic: Retain until the affected officer''s driving record has been updated with an "L1" indicator. Destroy after verification or one calendar year.

series_description: This record series consists of original computer printouts (inventories) of all Ohio Department of Public Safety divisional entities (which includes Ohio Emergency Management Agency, Ohio Emergency Medical Services, Ohio Bureau of Motor Vehicles, Ohio State Highway Patrol, Ohio Investigative Unit, Ohio Homeland Security, Ohio Narcotics Intelligence Center, and Office of Criminal Justice Services) transfer documents (HP-74, DPS0116), emails and correspondence, salvage forms, physical inventory forms, DAS surplus and/or trade-in turn-in document form.
retention_statement: Paper: Records listed in the Records Series Description shall be kept in office for five (5) years and ; Electronic: Records listed in the Records Series Description shall be kept digitally for a period of seven (7) years.

series_description: This record series contains traffic crash report form OH-1 and photographs, and may contain traffic crash report forms OH-2, OH-3, and OH-1P, crash reconstruction forms, HP-3A (OHP 0041), HP-12 (OHP 0086), HP-25D (OHP 1567), HP-28 (OHP 0161), HP-70A (OHP 0269), HP-70-B (OHP 0271), coroner reports, toxicology reports,and any other supporting documentation generated by the State Highway Patrol during investigations of traffic crashes. These records are retained electronically in document imaging and storing systems.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and validated ; Electronic: Retain current year plus five years from the date the crash was reported.

series_description: These records capture information from reports generated by the multi-agency Drug Recognition Experts (DRE) when evaluating suspects/subjects suspected of using controlled substances in violation of the law. The reports are generated from tablets issued to each officer in the program and are stored on a remote server by a contracted vendor.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five (5) years plus the current year. Reports are held on the server of a contracted vendor funded through the National Highway Transportation and Safety Administration (NHTSA).

series_description: This record series contains all documentation related to a customer''s request to cancel his or her vehicle registration including: BMV Form 4311 (Customer Request to Cancel Registration), BMV Form 4870 (Plate Cancellation Complete/Court Reject Notification), and any additional correspondence used to process the cancellation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until imaged and validated in the Custom Processing Imaging Client (CPIC) system ; Electronic: Retain in BMV''s Custom Processing Imaging Client (CPIC) system for three years following date of cancellation or rejection.

series_description: Backgrounds (Criminal and/or Record Checks) are conducted for various State of Ohio agencies and boards & commissions, and for contractors/consultants contracted to work at ODPS facilities throughout Ohio, and for individuals wishing to enlist in a branch of the U.S. military. This record series may include, but is not limited to, the following: the request for criminal and/or record checks; applicant consent/release forms; supporting documentation presented at application; DD369 Police Record Check forms from U.S. military recruiting stations; criminal records obtained from law enforcement agencies and various courts; State (BCI) and/or Nationwide (FBI) criminal records; applicant’s fingerprints; driving records; data and documents collected during field interviews; background and polygraph result summaries; medical/psychological screening results; and letter/memo of approval or denial. Upon completion of the background, results are transmitted to the Requester, which shall become the official record. A copy of the record is retained in accordance with this schedule for the purpose of receipt confirmation and auditing purposes. NOTES: Backgrounds for employment at ODPS are retained in accordance with “Backgrounds and background checks for Employment” SAN 760-0788 (for hired applicants), “Application for Employment Bid Folders (Applicant Jackets)” SAN 760-1492 (for rejected applicants), or “Contract for goods, services, and property leases” SAN 760-1483 for contractors/vendors.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 30 days after the date the record is transmitted to the Requester. ; Electronic: Retain on ODPS network for two (2) years, plus current.
