series_description: Any audio and/or video recordings made for administrative or operational purposes including, but not limited to, telephone calls, radio transmissions, interviews or interrogations, in-car or body-worn camera footage, training footage, etc. There are three (3) categories of audio/visual recordings: 1) Routine audio recordings of phone and radio transmissions will be held for ninety (90) days; routine audio/video recordings and dash cam footage not used in a court case are to be retained for one hundred eighty (180) days, (with the exception of security videos which are retained only thirty (30) days); and training videos will be retained as needed, then purged/deleted provided they are no longer of administrative value. 2) Evidentiary audio and/or video recordings used in case investigations or court proceedings are to be retained for two (2) years or until criminal, civil, and/or administrative proceedings are complete, whichever is longer, then destroyed provided they are no longer of administrative or historical value. 3) Non-evidentiary audio and/or video recordings of major incidents, including recordings that are subpoenaed, are to be retained until criminal, civil, and/or administrative proceedings are complete, then destroyed provided they are no longer of administrative value. Retention justification for audio/visual recordings are as follows: The ninety (90) day and one hundred eighty (180) day retention periods will allow enough time for requests for public records and administrative reviews. The two year retention is for litigation purposes. The thirty (30) day retention period for security videos is the maximum time allowed due to limited server space. There are many other situations involving audio and video recordings where there may be a need to retain certain recordings longer than the established retention schedule. Court proceedings may be indefinite, or in some instances, recordings may be retained permanently for historical value and/or training purposes.
retention_statement: Electronic: The Ohio State Highway Patrol retains different categories of audio and/or video recordings: 1) Routine, 2) Evidentiary, and 3) Non-evidentiary audio and/or video recordings. Please see “Record Series Description” for applicable retention periods.

series_description: This record series is the official record collected and retained pursuant to employment and for individuals who have logical or physical access to Department of Public Safety (DPS) systems / areas containing Criminal Justice Information (CJI). Records may include, but are not limited to the following: the request for background; applicant consent / release forms; criminal records checks; Criminal History Record check response letters; National WebCheck waiver forms; driving records; applicant fingerprints; credit histories; supporting documentation presented at application; data and documents collected during field interviews; the background and polygraph results summaries; medical / psychological screening results; and the form / memo of approval or denial. Upon the decision to hire, or if applicable, upon academy class graduation, Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSHP) / Ohio Investigative Unit employee backgrounds are forwarded to OSHP Central Records and all remaining DPS employee backgrounds are forwarded to, and retained in DPS Human Resource Management in accordance with this schedule. Records may be transferred to the DPS Record Center. NOTES: 1) Backgrounds and background checks for all rejected applicants are retained in accordance with Series Authorization Number (SAN) 760-1492, “Application for Employment Bid Folders”; 2) Polygraphs for employment are retained in accordance with SAN 760-0773; 3) This record retention schedule also applies to OSHP auxiliary member applicant background records.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain seven (7) years from date of hire or academy class graduation, or completion date of finger-based criminal background checks, as applicable. ; Paper: Retain seven (7) years from date of hire or academy class graduation, or date of finger-based criminal background checks, as applicable. Records may be imaged, with exception of Criminal History Check response letters unless they meet the standards set forth in Section 5.10.1.2.2 “Encryption for CJI at Rest” in the Law Enforcement Automated Data Systems (LEADS) Security Policy. Paper records may be destroyed when electronic records are validated.

Digital media refers to photos and video; paper records include flight logs and Standard Operating Procedures for the Critical Infrastructure’s sUAS program. Some records may fulfill Homeland Security purposes and may be restricted.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five (5) years ; Electronic: Retain five (5) years

series_description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) Criminal Justice Information Services (CJIS) Security Policy requires any individual with access to unencrypted Criminal Justice Information (CJI) to be screened for arrest records. Those with a record must be submitted for approval prior to be given access to information obtained through LEADS. This information must be kept by LEADS during the duration of an individual's employment career to document that LEADS was notified of the arrest and to reference during future notifications.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan and upload ; Electronic: Retain until subject reaches the age of 80 or subject is deceased, whichever occurs first.

series_description: The Mobile Field Force (MFF) Mission Files consist of any or all the following form documents and any supporting documentation used to compile the forms or to document any functions related to the forms or missions: Mobile Field Force Warning Order (OHP 0010) and Mobile Field Force Operations Order (OHP 0011).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain current, plus two (2) years following execution of the operation and closing of the file. Records are retained in the Office of Field Operations – MFF. Paper files may be destroyed once scanned and saved to file. ; Electronic: Retain current, plus two (2) years following execution of the operation and closing of the file. Records are retained in the Office of Field Operations – MFF.

series_description: Record series includes all paper or digital application materials, to include nomination forms, application forms, non-disclosure agreements, and all supporting documents that are scanned or emailed and electronically stored in the shared drive for program administration purposes. Includes HLS 0210, HLS 0211, and HLS 0233.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until stored electronically ; Electronic: Retain five (5) years

series_title: Medical Transportation Forms for Application and Inspection
series_description: This record series includes applications, supplementary forms, reports, and supporting documents and correspondence created and received during Division of Emergency Medical Service (EMS) / Medical Transportation section transactions for licensure and license renewal.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper records including initial and renewal applications and related documents received before February 4, 2020 are maintained by the Division of EMS through the end of the calendar year from the date received and for an additional four (4) calendar years ; Electronic: Information submitted via the Medical Transportation Licensing System (MTLS) on-line application and renewal licensure and inspection reporting system is maintained through the current calendar year from the effective date plus an additional (4) calendar years, reviewed and approved for by medical transportation staff

series_description: Stroke protocols submitted annually to the Division of Emergency Medical Services by local agencies pursuant to
retention_statement: Paper: If received in paper format, the item will be scanned and converted to machine readable medium and the paper destroyed immediately. ; Electronic: Each version will be maintained no longer than two (2) years from the end of the calendar year following the date of submission to the Division.

series_description: The Transcript Processing System is used to monitor record requests submitted by courts, prosecuting attorneys, and law enforcement agencies. The system tracks name, Ohio driver license numbers, SSN (truncated upon submission), and date of birth.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain three (3) years from date Received Date.

series_title: Ohio Narcotics and Intelligence Center- Forensic Storage
series_description: To set a retention schedule for digital device extractions and supporting documentation of evidence from criminal cases.
retention_statement: Electronic: Two (2) years plus current year. ; Paper: Two (2) years plus current year.

series_description: OIU Agents’ application materials for covert IDs and all other covert identification materials. During employment and upon separation, the materials should be kept separate from an Agent’s personnel file to maintain confidentiality.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for the length of employment plus one year after separation. ; Electronic: Retain for the length of employment plus one year after separation.

series_description: This series contains records of the service tickets logged for customers arrived/queue BMV Agencies for the services like Driver’s License, Vehicle Registrations, etc… . Data includes basic information for the customer such as name, phone number, email address and history of the event/actions on the ticket like queue timings and communications via Short Message Service (SMS) or emails. NOTE: There is no system integration between Q-flow and BASS or any other systems.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain three (3) years from the date case/ticket was created; thirty (30) days for customer communications, SMS and/or emails.

series_description: This record series contains information and supporting documentation regarding reasonable suspicion of criminal conduct or activity submitted to the Ohio Narcotics Intelligence Center (ONIC). Records include source and method of receipt of information; documented assessment of relevancy in accordance with “Title 28 - Code of Federal Regulations – Part 23” (
retention_statement: Paper: Records are required to meet submission criteria for criminal intelligence information (i.e., “validated”) as set forth in 28 CFR 23 prior to retention. Validated records shall be retained for no more than five (5) years. Pursuant to 28 CFR 23, records shall be periodically reviewed (i.e., “purge review”) during retention for continued compliance to 28 CFR 23 submission criteria. If a record is reviewed and validated as continuing to meet submission criteria, it will receive a new maximum retention period of 5 years. Records found to longer meet the criteria shall be purged immediately. ; Electronic: Records are required to meet submission criteria for criminal intelligence information (i.e., “validated”) as set forth in 28 CFR 23 prior to retention. Validated records shall be retained in the ONIC’s record management system for no more than five (5) years. Pursuant to 28 CFR 23, records shall be periodically reviewed (i.e., “purge review”) during retention for continued compliance to 28 CFR 23 submission criteria. If a record is reviewed and validated as continuing to meet submission criteria, it will receive a new maximum retention period of 5 years. Records found to longer meet the criteria shall be purged immediately.

series_description: This record series contains requests for assistance with investigations, assessments, and event planning that are submitted to the Ohio Narcotics Intelligence Center (ONIC). Records include source and method of receipt of requests; the final work product and/or response, e.g., bulletins, profiles, and case reports; and as applicable, dissemination to appropriate law enforcement and/or public safety agencies. NOTE: If any request is evaluated and found to meet the threshold of reasonable suspicion of crimiinal activity set forth in “Title 28 - Code of Federal Regulations – Part 23” (
retention_statement: Paper: Records are retained for ten (10) years. ; Electronic: Retain in ONIC’s record management system for ten (10) years

series_description: The Government Access Site is a role-based web site used by external government agencies, courts, and peace officers. Users with access to the system (depending on role) can view BMV reinstatement requirements, three year driving abstracts, driving history, print mailing labels to mail items to the BMV (Plates/Licenses), vehicle registration records, and vehicle title records.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain records thirteen (13) months

series_title: Investigatory Case Files (CASES CLOSED) and Medical Transportation Complaints
series_description: In accordance with sections 4765.101, 4765.11, 4765.15, 4765.17, 4766.08, and 4766.11 of the Ohio Revised Code (ORC), the State Board of Emergency Medical, Fire, and Transportation Services (via the Division of EMS) may investigate and take disciplinary action against a certificate-holder, license-holder, permit-holder, charter-holder, EMS certificate of accreditation-holder, or EMS certificate of approval-holder who violates any section of ORC or Ohio Administrative Code pertaining to their certificate, license, permit, charter, certificate of accreditation, or certificate of approval. The case file will include all documents gathered as a part of the investigation into alleged violations including: findings, information regarding hearings, recommended disciplinary actions and appeals; dispositions; adjudications; and consent agreements. Limited information regarding case (such as name, case number, final action) is maintained in electronic databases.
retention_statement: Paper: Active case files are maintained at the Division of EMS until closed. Closed cases are retained for six (6) years from the date closed and ; Electronic: Active case files are maintained at the Division of EMS until closed. Closed cases are retained for six (6) years from the date closed and

series_description: This form will document informal corrective counseling or training of employees when the conduct or issue does not rise to the level of an administrative investigation. This shall be used when the counseling or training was not ordered by the Office of Personnel as a result of discipline or a correction imposed as a result of an administrative investigation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three (3) years, plus current year. Paper version should be scanned into the Employee Incident Reporting tool and shredded once scanned. ; Electronic: Retain three (3) years, plus current year.

series_description: Documents the repairs, replacement, and any other work done by the Division armorers.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two (2) years, plus current year. ; Electronic: Retain two (2) years, plus current year.

series_description: The Project Tracking System tracks phone calls taken by the Ohio BMV Deputy Technical Services Unit, License Control Unit, Telecommunications Section, and Vehicle Information Services Section. The system contains confidential personal information (Name, address, Ohio driver license/state ID number, SSN, and date of birth). Access to the system is limited and assigned by BMV management. The system allows BMV Sections to assign calls to other BMV sections.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain records for twenty-four (24) months

series_title: Office of Criminal Justice Services (OCJS) State Grant Files
series_description: All financial and programmatic records related to the receipt and expenditure of state grant funding including, but not limited to, grant applications and supporting documentation; award and acceptance forms; financial reports including budgets, cash requests, expenditures, and source documentation; records of purchase and/or acquisition; project information; use agreements; and relevant audit reports.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain three (3) years from the date of submission of the final expenditure report. ; Paper: Retain three (3) years from the date of submission of the final expenditure report.

series_description: Record series contain records related to all Department of Public Safety (DPS) Information Technology consultants, which may include, but not limited to: the IT Consultant handbook with signoff, the form located within DAS’ HR-50 Guidelines for Contracted Resources, CJIS security addendum signoff, and the H1B letter to renew a visa.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain six (6) years from last date of service to the agency ; Electronic: Retain six (6) years from last date of service to the agency

series_title: Ohio Traffic Safety Office (OTSO) “Materials Order Form”
series_description: This records series contains completed traffic safety material order forms (OTS 0001) received from government entities, organizations, and the general public processed by the OTSO.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain all forms received one year plus current year ; Electronic: Retain all forms received one year plus current year

series_title: Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSP) Non-Criminal Request For Service (RFS)
series_description: This record series contains requests for service/assistance that are non-criminal in nature (e.g., request for a phone number for an abandoned vehicle, next of kin request or simple traffic violation; tips and leads or reports of suspicious behavior that do not meet the standard for a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) or criminal request for service (RFS)).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in electronic data-system for five (5) years and

series_description: This record series contains manuals produced or obtained by agency personnel for internal personnel training or reference. Course curriculum and supporting documents for DPS developed training courses are retained under 760-1265, field training guides are retained under 760-0412, and employee training records are kept under 760-1538.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_title: Leasing and Land Management Property Leases and Records
series_description: The records retained in this series include all Contracts, Leases, and records pertaining to real estate rentals for the Ohio Department of Public Safety and include: 1) The original signed lease and addendums; 2) The original signed construction contract; 3) copies of all bids, proposal or quotes for projects; 4) Release and Permit information (if required); 5) copies of Controlling Board authorizations; 6) Vendor complaint documentation; 7) copies of issued purchase order; 8) copy of Property Deeds (original filed with county auditor); 9) copy of Easements (original filed with county auditor); 10) Other related documentation, correspondence and support information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain current plus six years from the date of expiration or cancellation of related lease. ; Paper: Retain current plus six years from the date of expiration or cancellation of related lease.

series_title: Applications for waiver of testing-CDL licensing for current & former military personnel
series_description: This record series consists of BMV 2070 "Application for Skills Test Military Exception" and BMV 2071 "Application for Military Even Exchange Waiver" forms. Both forms provide similar program of benefits for military waivers for CDL testing for qualified current and former military personnel. The forms may contain the person’s full name, address, city, state, zip code, date of birth, Driver’s license number, and military service information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in the BMV database for two years plus current year and ; Paper: Retain completed document until after verification of imaged documents

series_description: Electronic files pertaining to the Ohio Peace Officer Training classes conducted by the OSHP Training Academy according to the Ohio Peace OfficerTraining Academy (OPOTA) Standards. The files include the roster of basic trainees on the first day of class, the final roster before graduation, the roster of trainees who graduate, the final OPOTA exam scores, and the OPOTA curriculum to the particular class.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper files are destroyed upon scanning. ; Electronic: Retain in electronic files thirty (30) years from the date of graduation

series_description: This record series contains all identification and medical records for Division-owned canines including registration license, passport (if applicable), veterinary and vaccination records, and other non-training records.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan all paper documents into OTIS and destroy ; Electronic: Retain for two (2) years after the canine is no longer in service with the Division

series_description: This record series contains a record of citizen complaints, investigative artifacts for complaints, journal entries, and supervisor complaint reviews and notes
retention_statement: Paper: All supplimental documents should be scanned into EIR and destroyed ; Electronic: Retain 5 years plus current. Complaints will be automatically purged by EIR system

series_description: This record series contains all documentation related to the administrative review of state-owned patrol car crashes. Contains HP-3C, repairs/damage estimates, supervisor reviews, vehicle details, template 12, OH-3, and other relevant crash information involving patrol cars. All documentation shall be stored in the Employee Incident Reporting (EIR) database
retention_statement: Paper: Scan all paper documents into EIR and destroy ; Electronic: Retain for two (2) years plus current

series_title: eWarrants - Warrant and Protection Order Information
series_description: This record documents the transactions, record inquires, file uploads, and data entries performed by users of the eWarrants application, which is electronic warrant and protection order tool used by criminal justice agencies to facilitate the entry, authorization, and service of records from court into Law Enforcement Automated Data System (LEADS) eWarrants contains supplemental information, included but not limited to, Computerized Criminal History and Driver information Data obtained from LEADS uploaded as a file attachment for law enforcement purposes
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for six (6) years after a record is served, recalled, or expired

series_description: Includes, but not limited to, DHS audit questionnaires, checklists, responses, attachments, and findings associated with audits of the SAIC/STACC's administration of the DHS Classified Security Information (CNSI) Program
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for current, plus two (2) years ; Electronic: Retain for current, plus two (2) years

series_title: STACC/SAIC SOPs/Policies for Administering the DHS CNSI Program
series_description: Includes, but not limited to, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), policies, internal operating guidelines, and other administrative documents (e.g. Duty Officer schedule, EOD schedule, STE/IDS test results) setting forth the operation of the DHS Classified National Security Information (CNSI) Program, which is administered by the STACC/SAIC
retention_statement: Paper: Retain most updated version until superseded or obsolete ; Electronic: Retain most updated version until superseded or obsolete.

series_description: Includes records related to the operations of the DHS-approved Secure Rooms (SR) and Secure briefing space at the STACC and SAIC, including attendance and access rights for such rooms. Records include, but not limited to: passwords (e.g. SF 700) and PINs; security checks (e.g., SF 701, 702); secure equipment and inventory documents; attendance records and logs; security-related training s; SF 312 debriefing/inactivation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in OHS network drive current, plus two (2) years. ; Paper: Retain in office files current, plus two (2) years

series_description: Documents allowing access to classified information, and compliance with DHS security protocols, in furtherance of the DHS Classified National Security Information (CNSI) program, administered by the STACC/SAIC. This includes, but not limited to: SVTC and briefing rosters, VARs, Perm Cert approvals visitor logs and sign-in sheets, DPS badging requests, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one (1) year ; Electronic: Retain for one (1) year

series_description: Includes documents related to obtaining and maintaining a DHS-sponsored Security Clearance, in furthermore of the DHS Classified National Security Information (CNSI) Program, administered by the STACC/SAIC. Documents include but not limited to the nomination forms, supporting documents (e.g. fingerprints, eAPP, SF 312) HSDN access and PINS, and self-reporting documentation (e.g. changes in personal status, foreign contacts, foreign travel).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office files throughout the length of the individual's security clearance (10 year max for initial issuance, followed by 10 year max for each renewal/Periodic Review ; Electronic: Retain in OHS network drive throughout the length of the individual's security clearance (10 year max for initial issuance, followed by 10 year max for each renewal/Periodic Review)

series_description: Includes design development, programmatic documentation, architect/mechanical drawings, architect agreements, contracts, change orders, construction reports (ie.. meeting minutes, sign in sheets, drawing review comments, project manuals, equipment lists, contractor cost proposals, project budget, capital budget data, project correspondence, vendor compliance, bid documents and fiscal documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years after building or facility is destroyed, sold, or otherwise vacated ; Electronic: Retain 2 years after building or facility is destroyed, sold, or otherwise vacated

series_description: Contains all photographic evidence collected during the course of a commercial vehicle inspection documenting any violations. For video recordings, see 760-0826.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 5 years plus current

series_title: Drug Recognition Expert Applications and Training Records
series_description: Contains the applications for selected Drug Recognition Experts (DRE) statewide, initial training records, continuing education and work product documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for length of service as a DRE plus two years.

series_description: The Quality Assurance Unit of the Ohio Bureau of Criminal Investigations (BCI) conducts quality assurance audits on WebCheck agencies to ensure compliance with state and federal statutes regarding the submission and dissemination of fingerprint-based criminal backgrounds, as well as, ensuring compliance with BCI guidelines regarding the use of WebCheck equipment used to complete fingerprint-based criminal backgrounds. Audits may be conducted on records containing BCI and/or FBI results every year. Audits on the use of WebCheck equipment located in the Office of Human Resources, Criminal Records Unit, are conducted every 3 years. Records may include, but are not limited to, fingerprint transactions submitted through WebCheck equipment, background checks of individuals operating equipment, MOU between WebCheck agency and BCI, Waiver agreements, Dissemination Logs, Policies regarding record storage of Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) and policies for misuse of CHRI, results of audit, correspondence between BCI and the Ohio Department of Public Safety, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years from the close of the audit. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years from the close of the audit.

series_description: Contains the current and historical version of the LEADS Manual.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for six (6) years

series_description: Research requested by external sources regarding programs, divisions, and information the Ohio Traffic Safety Office considers key areas of focus.
retention_statement: Paper: 10 business days ; Electronic: 10 business days

series_description: Various payment types (checks, credit cards, etc.) issued for payment of services are returned by the bank and remain unpaid. The Tax Distribution Section is notified, and a letter is sent to the customer requesting payment. If payment is not received within 45 days, the claim is forwarded to the office of the Attorney General for collection assistance.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until the returned item has been paid in full and cleared through the office of the Attorney General. Destroy 2 years thereafter providing audited by Auditor of the State and audit report released. If unpaid, retain for 60 years from the date entered into the Returned Payment System
